+# EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+
+**Summary:** Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Stories:** STORY-0001, STORY-0002, STORY-0003, STORY-0004, STORY-0005, STORY-0006, STORY-0007, STORY-0008, STORY-0009, STORY-0010, STORY-0011, STORY-0012, STORY-0013, STORY-0014, STORY-0015, STORY-0016, STORY-0017, STORY-0018, STORY-0019, STORY-0020, STORY-0021, STORY-0022, STORY-0023, STORY-0024, STORY-0025, STORY-0026, STORY-0027, STORY-0028, STORY-0029, STORY-0030, STORY-0031
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md`, `specs/specs/domains/input.md`, `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md`
+**Status:** 0/31 done
+
+## STORY-0001
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Capture paste session frame
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** to read the focused element position, size, and window attributes during a paste session
+**So that** it can snapshot the frontmost app and focused element state at paste time
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Paste session capture reads the focused element position, size, and window attributes from the Accessibility API.
+- AC-2: The captured session state includes enough information to resolve the element frame and its window.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:14-29`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0002
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Recognize text entry roles
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** to treat text field, text area, and combo box roles as text entry roles
+**So that** paste target heuristics can identify common editable controls
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The paste target detector treats `kAXTextFieldRole` as a text entry role.
+- AC-2: The paste target detector treats `kAXTextAreaRole` as a text entry role.
+- AC-3: The paste target detector treats `kAXComboBoxRole` as a text entry role.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:31-37`
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0003
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Observe focus notifications and reinstall observers
+
+**As a** the accessibility monitor
+**I want** to observe focused UI element and focused window changes, then swap observers when the frontmost app changes
+**So that** focus tracking stays aligned with the active application
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The observer listens for kAXFocusedUIElementChangedNotification and kAXFocusedWindowChangedNotification.
+- AC-2: For each tracked PID, the monitor creates an AXObserver, registers the notifications, adds it to the main run loop in common modes, and enables manual accessibility.
+- AC-3: When a different application becomes frontmost, the previous observer is uninstalled and a new observer is installed for the new process.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:39-60`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0004
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Reject disabled and unfocused paste targets
+
+**As a** the accessibility detector
+**I want** to exclude disabled elements and require stronger signals when no focused ancestor exists
+**So that** only eligible text-entry controls are treated as paste targets
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If kAXEnabledAttribute is false, the detector returns false.
+- AC-2: When an element has no focused ancestor, it qualifies only if it has both a selected text range and a settable value.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:61-66`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0005
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Apply focused paste heuristics and tree search
+
+**As a** the accessibility detector
+**I want** to use relaxed heuristics when focus is present and search the tree depth-first with focused children first
+**So that** focused controls can still be accepted even when the direct element is nested
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When an element or any ancestor is focused, the detector accepts the element if AXEditable is true, AXValue is settable, it has a text entry role and a selected text range, or it has a text entry role alone.
+- AC-2: Tree traversal is depth-first, focused children are checked before non-focused children, focus context propagates to descendants, and traversal stops at depth 12.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:67-72`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0006
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Resolve paste targets by priority
+
+**As a** the accessibility detector
+**I want** to prefer the direct focused element, then cached observation, then fallback discovery
+**So that** the result always comes from the most trusted available source
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the direct focused element query succeeds and the element is a paste target, the detector returns true.
+- AC-2: If the direct focused element exists but is not a paste target, the detector returns false.
+- AC-3: If no direct focused element is available, cached paste target observation is accepted only when PID and window ID match the frontmost app and the cached status is editable.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:71-72`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0007
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Extract focused element text
+
+**As a** the accessibility reader
+**I want** to read focused element text from AXValue, then text markers, then ancestor elements
+**So that** post-paste learning can inspect the current text content
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The reader first attempts to read kAXValueAttribute as a String or NSAttributedString.
+- AC-2: If value reading fails, the reader attempts AXTextMarkerRange-based extraction.
+- AC-3: If the focused element has no text, the reader walks up to 8 ancestors.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:73-80`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0008
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Allow external suspension
+
+**As a** monitor controller
+**I want** monitoring to be externally suspended and resumed
+**So that** shortcut capture and similar flows can pause input processing
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Monitoring can be externally suspended.
+- AC-2: Monitoring can be externally resumed.
+- AC-3: Suspension is used during shortcut capture.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:283-297`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0009
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Stop the monitor cleanly
+
+**As a** monitor controller
+**I want** stopping the monitor to tear down all runtime state
+**So that** no background input processing survives shutdown
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Stopping the monitor removes the event tap.
+- AC-2: Stopping the monitor halts the background thread's run loop.
+- AC-3: Stopping the monitor resets the chord engine.
+- AC-4: Stopping the monitor resets the suspension flag.
+- AC-5: Stopping the monitor resets the key release guard.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:283-297`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0010
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Treat start as idempotent
+
+**As a** monitor controller
+**I want** starting an already active monitor to succeed immediately
+**So that** duplicate start calls do not reinstall the monitor
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Starting the monitor when it is already active returns success immediately.
+- AC-2: Starting an already active monitor does not reinstall the monitor.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:283-297`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0011
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Check pasteability before pasting
+
+**As a** paste target detector
+**I want** the system to determine whether the focused UI element can accept text before simulating paste
+**So that** paste is only attempted against suitable targets
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Before simulating a paste, the system determines whether the focused UI element can accept text.
+- AC-2: The check uses AXUIElement to inspect the frontmost application's focused element.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0012
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Prefer direct focused-element results
+
+**As a** paste target detector
+**I want** direct Accessibility queries to win over cached observations
+**So that** current focus state is trusted first
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If a direct query returns a focused element that is a paste target, it is accepted immediately.
+- AC-2: If a direct query returns a focused element that is not a paste target, it is rejected immediately.
+- AC-3: A direct non-target result overrides cached data.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0013
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Use cached observations only as fallback
+
+**As a** paste target detector
+**I want** cached paste-target observations to be used only when no direct focused element is available
+**So that** the fallback respects the current frontmost application
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If no direct focused element is available, the cached observation may be used.
+- AC-2: The cached observation is accepted only when its process ID and window ID match the current frontmost application.
+- AC-3: The cached observation is accepted only when its cached status is editable.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0014
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Traverse the accessibility tree recursively
+
+**As a** paste target detector
+**I want** candidate elements to be checked through a depth-first accessibility walk
+**So that** nested controls can still be recognized as paste targets
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The accessibility tree is walked depth-first to a maximum depth of 12 levels.
+- AC-2: Focused children are checked before non-focused children.
+- AC-3: Once any ancestor is identified as focused, focus context propagates downward to descendants.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0015
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Reject disabled or under-specified elements
+
+**As a** paste target detector
+**I want** disabled elements and unfocused elements without enough metadata to be rejected
+**So that** only genuine text-entry controls qualify
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Disabled elements are never paste targets.
+- AC-2: Without focus context, an element must have both a selected text range attribute and a settable value attribute to be accepted.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0016
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Accept focused editable text targets
+
+**As a** paste target detector
+**I want** focused editable elements and text-entry controls to qualify as paste targets
+**So that** normal text fields are recognized reliably
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: With focus context, an element is accepted if its editable attribute is true.
+- AC-2: With focus context, an element is accepted if its value attribute is settable.
+- AC-3: With focus context, an element is accepted if it has a text entry role and a selected text range.
+- AC-4: With focus context, an element is accepted if it has a text entry role alone.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:360-401`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0017
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Track the active paste target
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** continuously cache whether the frontmost application's focused element is an editable paste target
+**So that** the paste flow can fall back to the cached observation when the direct Accessibility API query has no focused element
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The monitor keeps a cached editable or non-editable paste-target observation for the frontmost application.
+- AC-2: When the direct Accessibility API query returns no focused element, paste-target detection uses the cached observation as a fallback.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:403-423`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0018
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Switch observers on app activation
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** replace the accessibility observer when the frontmost application changes
+**So that** observations always apply to the active application
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a different application becomes frontmost, the previous accessibility observer is torn down.
+- AC-2: When a different application becomes frontmost, a new observer is installed for the newly active application.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:403-423`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0019
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Install focused-element observers
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** create the required accessibility observer for each tracked application
+**So that** focus changes can be observed reliably
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each tracked application has an AXObserver that watches for focused UI element changed notifications.
+- AC-2: Each tracked application has an AXObserver that watches for focused window changed notifications.
+- AC-3: The observer is attached to the main run loop.
+- AC-4: On installation, enhanced accessibility is enabled on the application element.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:403-423`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0020
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Record paste-target observations
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** record editable and non-editable focus observations from accessibility notifications
+**So that** paste decisions can verify whether the cached observation applies to the current frontmost window
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a focus change notification fires and the notified element is a paste target, the recorded status is editable.
+- AC-2: When a focus or window change notification fires and the element is not a paste target, the recorded status is non-editable.
+- AC-3: Other notification types are ignored.
+- AC-4: Each recorded observation includes the process ID and, when available, the window ID.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:403-423`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0021
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Maintain paste target monitoring and focused element resolution
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** paste target monitoring and focused element resolution to stay synchronized and trust direct queries over stale observations
+**So that** editable-state tracking reflects the current frontmost application
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The paste target monitor is a singleton that runs for the lifetime of the application.
+- AC-2: On application activation, the monitor uninstalls any existing accessibility observer, clears the current paste target observation, and installs a new observer for the newly frontmost application's process.
+- AC-3: When installing an observer, the system ensures accessibility information is available even for applications that do not natively export it.
+- AC-4: The observer subscribes to focused UI element changed and focused window changed notifications and receives them during modal operations and tracking.
+- AC-5: Focus or window change notifications produce editable or non-editable observations, and other notification types are ignored.
+- AC-6: Direct focused-element query is used first; if it fails, the system performs a recursive depth-first search from the focused window and stops after at most 12 ancestor levels.
+- AC-7: Eligibility is determined from the documented accessibility properties: role, enabled state, editable state, focused state, selected text range presence, and whether the value attribute is settable.
+- AC-8: A directly queried focused element that is a paste target is accepted immediately and recorded in the monitor.
+- AC-9: A directly queried focused element that is not a paste target is rejected even if stored observations say otherwise.
+- AC-10: If no focused element can be queried directly, the monitor falls back to stored observation only when the observation matches the current process and window and has an editable status.
+- AC-11: The full paste target check sequence verifies accessibility permission first, then the frontmost application, then monitor synchronization, then the frontmost window identifier, then the directly focused element, and then the priority-based decision logic.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:326-370`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0022
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Extract focused text through ancestor walks
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** focused-element text extraction to walk up ancestor elements when the current element yields no text
+**So that** the system can recover text from nested controls
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Direct text extraction reads the value attribute and accepts both plain strings and attributed strings.
+- AC-2: Text marker extraction reads the start and end text markers, creates a range, and requests the string for that range.
+- AC-3: If neither direct text nor text marker text yields non-empty text, the system walks the parent chain.
+- AC-4: The ancestor walk tries both strategies at each ancestor and stops after at most 8 ancestor levels.
+- AC-5: The first non-empty trimmed text found at any level is returned.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:372-379`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0023
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Resolve the frontmost window from accessibility data
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** frontmost windows resolved from the system window list and window bounds
+**So that** accessibility features can work with the correct window frame
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The system queries the on-screen window list excluding desktop elements and selects the first window matching the process with layer 0 and alpha greater than 0.
+- AC-2: The window frame is extracted from the window bounds dictionary.
+- AC-3: If the bounds cannot be interpreted as a rectangle, that window is skipped.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0024
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Track the active paste target continuously
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a singleton monitor that tracks whether the focused element is editable for the active application
+**So that** paste behavior can react to focus changes in real time
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The monitor runs for the lifetime of the application.
+- AC-2: On application activation, the monitor uninstalls any existing accessibility observer, clears the current paste target observation, and installs a new observer for the newly active application process.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0025
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Subscribe to activation notifications on start
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the paste-target monitor to register for application activation notifications when it starts
+**So that** activation events can be observed and handled
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the monitor starts, it registers for application activation notifications from the operating system workspace.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:328-333`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0026
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Observe focus changes during interactive modes
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the accessibility observer to stay active across interactive modes
+**So that** focus changes are still detected during modal and tracking operations
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The observer is installed with accessibility information available even for applications that do not natively export it.
+- AC-2: The observer subscribes to focused UI element changed and focused window changed notifications.
+- AC-3: The observer receives notifications while the application is in interactive modes, including modal operations and tracking.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0027
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Classify accessibility focus observations
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** focus and window change notifications to be converted into editable or non-editable observations
+**So that** paste target state can be tracked consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Focus or window change notifications produce either an editable or non-editable observation.
+- AC-2: Other notification types are ignored when the element is not a paste target.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0028
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Resolve the focused element by fallback search
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** focused element resolution to try a direct query first and then search descendants if needed
+**So that** the system can find the active text input even when the direct query fails
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The application-level accessibility element is queried directly for its focused UI element first.
+- AC-2: If the direct query fails, the focused window is searched recursively depth-first for a focused descendant.
+- AC-3: The recursive search stops after a maximum depth of 12 levels.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0029
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Evaluate paste target eligibility from accessibility state
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** paste target eligibility to be decided from the element snapshot and priority rules
+**So that** directly focused elements can override stale background observations
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Eligibility is determined by inspecting role, enabled state, editable state, focused state, selected text range existence, and whether the value attribute is settable.
+- AC-2: If the directly queried focused element is a paste target, the check returns true immediately and records the observation.
+- AC-3: If the directly queried focused element exists but is not a paste target, the check returns false.
+- AC-4: If no focused element can be queried directly, the check falls back to the stored monitor observation and returns true only when it matches the current process and window and has editable status.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0030
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Run the full paste target check sequence
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** paste target checks to follow the required gating sequence
+**So that** the result is false when prerequisites are missing and the monitor stays synchronized
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Accessibility permission is checked first and the check returns false if it is not granted.
+- AC-2: The frontmost application is identified next and the check returns false if none exists.
+- AC-3: The monitor is started and synchronized before the frontmost window identifier is resolved.
+- AC-4: The directly focused element is queried and then evaluated using the priority-based decision logic.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0031
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-001 — Accessibility & Paste Targeting
+**Title:** Extract text from focused elements and ancestors
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** text extraction to try direct values, text markers, and ancestor fallbacks
+**So that** text can be read from both standard controls and web-like content views
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Direct text extraction reads the value attribute and accepts both plain strings and attributed strings.
+- AC-2: If direct text is unavailable, the system reads start and end text markers, creates a range, and requests the string for that range.
+- AC-3: If neither strategy yields non-empty text, the system walks up the parent chain and retries at each ancestor.
+- AC-4: The search stops after at most 8 ancestor levels and returns the first non-empty trimmed text found.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+
+**Summary:** Permissions & Privacy
+**Stories:** STORY-0032, STORY-0033, STORY-0034, STORY-0035, STORY-0036, STORY-0037, STORY-0038, STORY-0039, STORY-0040, STORY-0041, STORY-0042, STORY-0043, STORY-0044, STORY-0045, STORY-0046, STORY-0047, STORY-0048
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/correction-learning.md`
+**Status:** 0/17 done
+
+## STORY-0032
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Fail closed without accessibility permission
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** to check Accessibility trust with and without prompting
+**So that** paste target evaluation never runs without the required permission
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The permission check uses AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions with prompt false for a silent check and prompt true when the system permission dialog must be triggered.
+- AC-2: Paste target evaluation returns false immediately when accessibility permission is not granted.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:81-124`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0033
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Gate all Accessibility API operations
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** to avoid any Accessibility API operations unless Accessibility trust is granted
+**So that** observer creation, focus tracking, text extraction, and other AX calls only run when permission is available
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Every Accessibility API operation is guarded by Accessibility trust checks.
+- AC-2: When trust is not granted, the application does not attempt observer creation, focus tracking, text extraction, or other AX calls.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/accessibility-api.md:81-124`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0034
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Gate the pipeline on permissions and settings
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** to change pipeline availability from permission state and live settings changes
+**So that** the UI reflects current capabilities and reconfiguration happens immediately
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When microphone permission is denied, the status becomes error and the pipeline is unavailable.
+- AC-2: When accessibility permission is denied, the hotkey monitor fails and the status becomes error.
+- AC-3: When input monitoring permission is denied, the system degrades gracefully.
+- AC-4: When screen recording permission is denied, OCR features are unavailable.
+- AC-5: cleanupEnabled, frontmostWindowContextEnabled, postPasteLearningEnabled, ignoreOtherSpeakers, playSounds, speechModel, cleanupModel, and chordBindings write to UserDefaults and trigger live reconfiguration.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:19-21`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0035
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Require microphone permission at startup
+
+**As a** application
+**I want** to check and request microphone access using the macOS microphone APIs
+**So that** recording cannot start without microphone permission
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app checks microphone permission with `AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .audio)` and prompts with `AVCaptureDevice.requestAccess(for: .audio)`.
+- AC-2: If microphone permission is denied, initialization halts and the status transitions to an error with the message `Microphone access required`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:22-30`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0036
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Require Accessibility permission for hotkey monitoring
+
+**As a** application
+**I want** to check and request Accessibility access using the macOS Accessibility APIs
+**So that** the hotkey monitor can only start when Accessibility is granted
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app checks Accessibility permission with `AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions` using `prompt: false` and prompts with `prompt: true`.
+- AC-2: If Accessibility permission is denied, the hotkey monitor does not start and the status transitions to an error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:22-32`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0037
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Degrade gracefully without Input Monitoring
+
+**As a** application
+**I want** to check and request Input Monitoring access
+**So that** the app can continue operating with Accessibility alone
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app checks Input Monitoring with `IOHIDCheckAccess(kIOHIDRequestTypeListenEvent)` and prompts with `IOHIDRequestAccess(kIOHIDRequestTypeListenEvent)`.
+- AC-2: If Input Monitoring is denied, the app degrades gracefully and continues operating with Accessibility alone.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:34-41`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0038
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Handle Screen Recording permission loss
+
+**As a** application
+**I want** to check and request Screen Recording access
+**So that** OCR features are only available when Screen Recording is granted
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app checks Screen Recording with `CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess()` and prompts with `CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess()`.
+- AC-2: If Screen Recording is denied, OCR features are unavailable and inline recovery prompts are shown.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:34-41`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0039
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Open the correct System Settings pane for each permission
+
+**As a** application
+**I want** to open the exact System Settings URL for each privacy permission
+**So that** users are taken to the correct macOS privacy pane
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Input Monitoring opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_ListenEvent`.
+- AC-2: Screen Recording opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_ScreenCapture`.
+- AC-3: Accessibility opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Accessibility`.
+- AC-4: Microphone opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Microphone`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:43-55`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0040
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Enforce required and optional permissions
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to distinguish required privacy permissions from optional ones
+**So that** core functionality blocks when it must, while nonessential features degrade gracefully
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Microphone and Accessibility are treated as required permissions for core functionality.
+- AC-2: Input Monitoring and Screen Recording are treated as optional permissions.
+- AC-3: If Input Monitoring is denied, the app continues operating with accessibility-only behavior.
+- AC-4: If Screen Recording is denied, features gated by it present recovery prompts instead of preventing the app from continuing.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0041
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Provide accessibility checks and recovery
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to support both non-prompting and prompting accessibility checks
+**So that** it can verify trust without prompting and request trust when needed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The non-prompting accessibility check queries trusted-process status with the prompt option set to false and returns a boolean without displaying any dialog.
+- AC-2: The prompting accessibility request queries trusted-process status with the prompt option set to true and may show the operating system's accessibility permission dialog when the app is not trusted.
+- AC-3: The accessibility recovery path opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_Accessibility`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0042
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Surface accessibility failures during startup
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to react clearly when accessibility is missing
+**So that** I know why hotkey startup failed and what to do next
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the hotkey monitor fails to start, the app prompts for accessibility permission.
+- AC-2: In that case, the app sets the error message to `Accessibility access required -- grant permission then click Retry` and transitions to the error state.
+- AC-3: During initialization, if accessibility is not granted, the app opens the settings window and continues through audio pre-warming, model loading, and hotkey monitor startup.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0043
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Check and request input monitoring
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to check and request input monitoring permission through the HID subsystem
+**So that** keyboard event detection can be configured correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app checks input monitoring by querying the IOKit HID subsystem for listen-event access and comparing the result against the granted constant.
+- AC-2: The app requests input monitoring by calling the IOKit HID listen-event access request API, which may show a system dialog.
+- AC-3: The input monitoring recovery path opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_ListenEvent`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0044
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Fall back when input monitoring is denied
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to keep starting when input monitoring is unavailable
+**So that** accessibility-only operation can still work
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If input monitoring is not granted when the hotkey monitor is about to start, the app prompts for the permission but does not block startup.
+- AC-2: In that case, the app logs that input monitoring was not granted.
+- AC-3: In that case, the app attempts to operate with accessibility alone.
+- AC-4: During initialization, if input monitoring is not granted, the app opens the settings window alongside the accessibility check.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0045
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Manage screen recording permission state
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** screen recording permission to be tracked and refreshed consistently
+**So that** OCR-dependent features can respond to permission changes
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app checks screen recording permission with the operating system's preflight function and does not trigger a prompt during that check.
+- AC-2: The app requests screen recording permission with the operating system's access request function, which may display a system dialog.
+- AC-3: The recovery path opens `x-apple.systempreferences:com.apple.preference.security?Privacy_ScreenCapture`.
+- AC-4: The screen recording controller exposes granted and denied states, initializes by checking permission at construction time, refreshes by re-querying permission, and request-access triggers a request followed by an immediate refresh.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0046
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Relaunch after screen recording grant
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the onboarding flow to recover cleanly after granting screen recording
+**So that** the app still relaunches if macOS terminates it
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the user taps Enable for screen recording during onboarding, the app schedules a deferred relaunch by spawning a shell process that sleeps for 3 seconds and then opens the application bundle.
+- AC-2: After scheduling the deferred relaunch, the app requests screen recording access.
+- AC-3: After requesting access, the app opens the Screen Recording settings pane.
+- AC-4: If screen recording is enabled for `Use frontmost window OCR context` or `Learn from manual corrections after paste` but permission is denied, the settings window shows the inline recovery prompt `Ghost Pepper needs Screen Recording access. Grant it in System Settings, then return to Ghost Pepper.` and an `Open Screen Recording Settings` button.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:8-93`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0047
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Honor accessibility and screen recording permissions
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** permission handling to follow the specified behavior for accessibility and screen recording
+**So that** missing permissions are handled consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Accessibility permission is checked at entry and missing accessibility permission causes the relevant checks to return false or nil immediately.
+- AC-2: Screen recording remains an optional feature and capture is attempted regardless of screen-recording preflight status.
+- AC-3: When screen recording is unavailable and an error occurs, diagnostics include permission context only when relevant.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:318-400`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0048
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-002 — Permissions & Privacy
+**Title:** Handle missing screen recording permission
+

+## STORY-0049
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Handle hotkey start, stop, and restart
+
+**As a** the input system
+**I want** to fire callbacks that start recording, stop recording, and restart recording
+**So that** the core pipeline can control sessions from hotkeys
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each hotkey callback is a void closure.
+- AC-2: Start callbacks trigger recording start and trace creation.
+- AC-3: Stop callbacks trigger hotkey-lifted timestamp capture and the stop-and-transcribe flow.
+- AC-4: The restart callback clears or keeps the audio buffer based on the 16,000 sample threshold.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:7-14`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0050
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Install the global event tap with the required parameters
+
+**As a** hotkey monitor
+**I want** to listen for `flagsChanged`, `keyDown`, and `keyUp` events through the macOS global event tap
+**So that** the monitor receives keyboard state changes using the imposed CoreGraphics configuration
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The tap location is `.cgSessionEventTap`.
+- AC-2: The tap placement is `.tailAppendEventTap`.
+- AC-3: The tap mode is `.listenOnly`.
+- AC-4: The monitored event types are `flagsChanged`, `keyDown`, and `keyUp`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:99-111`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0051
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Require Accessibility permission for the event tap
+
+**As a** hotkey monitor
+**I want** to fail tap creation when Accessibility is denied
+**So that** the monitor does not start without the required privacy permission
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The tap requires Accessibility permission.
+- AC-2: If Accessibility is denied, tap creation fails and the monitor does not start.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:99-111`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0052
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Re-enable a disabled event tap immediately
+
+**As a** hotkey monitor
+**I want** to detect when macOS disables the event tap and turn it back on
+**So that** the monitor keeps working after timeout or input overload
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the operating system disables the event tap, the tap callback detects it.
+- AC-2: The tap is immediately re-enabled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:99-111`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0053
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Restart recording when push-to-talk upgrades to toggle
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** mid-recording push-to-talk upgrades to trigger a recording restart
+**So that** toggle-to-talk behavior takes effect immediately
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a push-to-talk chord is upgraded to a toggle-to-talk chord during recording, the system triggers a recording restart.
+- AC-2: On restart, if fewer than 16,000 samples have been accumulated, the audio buffer is cleared and the discarded sample count is logged.
+- AC-3: On restart, if 16,000 or more samples have been accumulated, the audio buffer is preserved and the log notes that existing audio is being kept.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:487-494`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0054
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Wire audio capture events into the performance trace
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** audio capture events to drive the performance trace and filtering pipeline
+**So that** recording timing and diarization input stay synchronized
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A recording started event records the microphone-live timestamp on the performance trace.
+- AC-2: A recording stopped event records the microphone-cold timestamp on the performance trace.

+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:535-556`

+- AC-2: A delivery completed event records the delivery-end timestamp and completes the performance trace.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:535-556`

+## STORY-0057
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring

+- AC-2: A push-to-talk stop event records the hotkey-lifted timestamp, stops recording, and starts transcription.

+- AC-4: A toggle-to-talk stop event records the hotkey-lifted timestamp, stops recording, and starts transcription.

+## STORY-0059

+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring

+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0066
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Gate non-modifier events before chord evaluation

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0067
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Derive modifier state from flags
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** modifier state to come from the event flag bitmask rather than from press/release toggles
+**So that** modifier tracking stays authoritative
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a modifier flag change event arrives, the set of currently held modifiers is derived from the event's flag bitmask.
+- AC-2: A modifier counts as pressed when its combined device-specific and generic bitmask is present in the current flags.
+- AC-3: The recognized modifier keys are Right Command, Left Command, Left Shift, Caps Lock, Left Option, Left Control, Right Shift, Right Option, Right Control, and Fn / Globe.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:7-70`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0068
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Compute pressed keys from the current event
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the authoritative pressed-key set to be derived from the event type and current flags
+**So that** the chord engine sees the correct key state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: For a modifier flag change event, the pressed-key set is the union of tracked non-modifier keys and modifiers derived from the new flags.
+- AC-2: For a key-down event, the pressed-key set is the union of tracked non-modifier keys, modifiers derived from the flags, and the newly pressed key.
+- AC-3: For a key-up event, the pressed-key set is tracked non-modifier keys minus the released key, unioned with modifiers derived from the flags.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:7-70`

+**Status:** pending

+

+**Title:** Recover pressed keys by polling

+**As a** system
+**I want** the chord engine to poll physical key state when evaluation produces no effects
+**So that** missed keys can be resynchronized
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When chord evaluation produces no effects, the system polls the physical state of all monitored keys using CGEvent source key state queries.
+- AC-2: If polling reveals pressed keys that are not currently tracked, the chord engine re-synchronizes with the union of tracked and polled keys.

+

+**Title:** Only add keys during polling recovery

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:7-70`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0071
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Require current-event agreement before poll corrections

+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring
+**Title:** Track engine state

+**Title:** Ignore no-op input events

+## STORY-0078
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-003 — Hotkey Monitoring

+# EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+
+**Summary:** Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Stories:** STORY-0094, STORY-0095, STORY-0096, STORY-0097, STORY-0098, STORY-0099, STORY-0100, STORY-0101, STORY-0102, STORY-0103, STORY-0104, STORY-0105, STORY-0106, STORY-0107, STORY-0108, STORY-0109, STORY-0110, STORY-0111, STORY-0112, STORY-0113, STORY-0114, STORY-0115, STORY-0116, STORY-0117, STORY-0118, STORY-0119, STORY-0120, STORY-0121
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md`, `specs/specs/domains/speech.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md`
+**Status:** 0/28 done
+
+## STORY-0094
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Record audio in the required format
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to start and stop the audio engine while preserving the recorder format
+**So that** recording produces a usable buffer for transcription
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Recording start clears the buffer, installs the tap, and starts the engine.
+- AC-2: Recording stop waits for the 200 ms flush delay, removes the tap, stops the engine, and returns a [Float] buffer.
+- AC-3: The recorder produces 16 kHz mono Float32 audio that matches the speech transcriber input format.
+- AC-4: Recording emits recording started, recording stopped, and converted audio chunk callbacks.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:7-18`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:32-36`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0095
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Fan out converted audio chunks
+
+**As a** the audio layer
+**I want** to deliver incremental converted audio chunks to both speech accumulation and diarization
+**So that** real-time transcription and speaker filtering can run together
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The converted audio chunk callback delivers incremental [Float] chunks.
+- AC-2: The recording session manager feeds the chunks into FluidAudio speech accumulation and Sortformer diarizer processing simultaneously.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:15-18`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0096
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Cancel short empty transcriptions
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** short recordings with no transcript to be dismissed quietly
+**So that** brief silence does not create unnecessary user-visible noise
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If transcription produces no text and the audio buffer contains fewer than 80,000 samples, the recording is silently cancelled.
+- AC-2: The only user-visible effect of that cancellation is dismissing the overlay.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:177-197`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0097
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Preserve empty-result bookkeeping
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** empty transcription results to still be archived and traced correctly
+**So that** the pipeline remains auditable even when no text is produced
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When no transcript is produced, the recording is still archived if the audio buffer is non-empty.
+- AC-2: The archive stores empty values for the raw and refined transcription fields when the transcript is empty.
+- AC-3: The performance trace is completed at the point where the empty result is detected rather than after delivery.
+- AC-4: The transcription end timestamp is still recorded even when the transcription result is empty.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:177-197`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0098
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Support optional audio engine pre-warm
+
+**As a** system optimizing recording startup
+**I want** an optional pre-warm operation for the audio engine
+**So that** the input processing chain can be initialized before recording if desired
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The audio engine supports an optional pre-warm operation.
+- AC-2: Pre-warm forces initialization of the input processing chain.
+- AC-3: Pre-warm prepares the engine for recording.
+- AC-4: Recording can start without pre-warming first.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:262-297`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0099
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Start a recording in the specified order
+
+**As a** recording pipeline
+**I want** recording start to execute its steps in order
+**So that** audio capture is initialized consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Starting a recording clears the sample buffer before any new audio is accumulated.
+- AC-2: Starting a recording queries the audio format from the engine's input on bus 0.
+- AC-3: Starting a recording logs the input device's sample rate and channel count.
+- AC-4: Starting a recording validates that sample rate and channel count are both greater than zero.
+- AC-5: If either input format validation check fails, a "no input available" error is reported.
+- AC-6: Starting a recording creates a format converter to 16 kHz mono 32-bit float.
+- AC-7: If converter creation fails, a "converter creation failed" error is reported.
+- AC-8: Starting a recording calculates the tap buffer size as approximately 100 milliseconds of input audio, truncated to an integer frame count.
+- AC-9: Starting a recording installs an audio tap on the input bus using the calculated buffer size and input format.
+- AC-10: Starting a recording starts the audio engine after the tap is installed.
+- AC-11: Starting a recording fires the recording-started notification after the engine starts.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:262-297`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0100
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Stop a recording in the specified order
+
+**As a** recording pipeline
+**I want** recording stop to execute its steps in order
+**So that** audio is flushed and finalized cleanly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Stopping a recording waits 200 milliseconds before teardown to allow buffered audio to drain.
+- AC-2: The 200 millisecond wait is asynchronous and cancellable.
+- AC-3: If the wait is cancelled, the remaining stop steps proceed immediately.
+- AC-4: Stopping a recording removes the audio tap from the input bus.
+- AC-5: Stopping a recording stops the engine after removing the tap.
+- AC-6: Stopping a recording fires the recording-stopped notification after the engine stops.
+- AC-7: Stopping a recording copies the accumulated sample buffer under lock and returns the copy.
+- AC-8: If the buffer is non-empty, stopping a recording logs the total sample count, equivalent duration in seconds, and maximum absolute amplitude.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:262-297`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0101
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Convert captured audio to the target format
+
+**As a** speech pipeline
+**I want** captured microphone buffers to be converted to the downstream target format
+**So that** the speech-to-text engine receives compatible audio
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: All captured audio is converted to 16,000 Hz, mono, 32-bit floating point, non-interleaved audio.
+- AC-2: For each input buffer, the output frame count is calculated from the target-to-input sample rate ratio applied to the input frame length, plus 1.
+- AC-3: If output buffer allocation fails, the buffer is skipped silently.
+- AC-4: Conversion uses a data-provider callback that supplies the input buffer on the first invocation and signals no data on subsequent invocations.
+- AC-5: If the converter reports an error, the error is logged and the buffer is dropped.
+- AC-6: If no float channel data is available or the converted frame length is zero, the buffer is skipped silently.
+- AC-7: Successfully converted frames are appended to the accumulation buffer with the thread-safe append operation.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:299-323`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0102
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Allocate a target-format output buffer before conversion
+
+**As a** speech pipeline
+**I want** each microphone buffer conversion to allocate a target-format output buffer using the calculated capacity
+**So that** conversion proceeds from a correctly sized destination buffer
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: For each microphone buffer, the system allocates an output buffer in the target format before conversion proceeds.
+- AC-2: The allocated output buffer uses the calculated output capacity.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:317-317`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0103
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Read converted samples from channel 0
+
+**As a** speech pipeline
+**I want** the converted buffer to be read from channel 0
+**So that** the intended mono audio channel is extracted
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When inspecting the converted buffer, the system reads the float channel data from channel 0.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:320-320`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0104
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Continue processing later buffers after a converter error
+
+**As a** speech pipeline
+**I want** converter errors on one buffer to not stop the stream
+**So that** later microphone buffers are still processed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the audio converter reports an error for one buffer, the system continues processing subsequent microphone buffers.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:318-320`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0105
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Manage the audio buffer with locking and dual delivery
+
+**As a** recording system
+**I want** the audio buffer to be stored and accessed safely
+**So that** recording data is consistent across threads
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A newly created recorder starts with an empty audio buffer.
+- AC-2: All buffer reads and writes are protected by a mutual exclusion lock.
+- AC-3: The lock is held only for the duration of the memory operation.
+- AC-4: Reset acquires the lock, empties the buffer, and releases the lock.
+- AC-5: Append acquires the lock, appends new frames, and releases the lock before firing the converted-audio-chunk notification.
+- AC-6: The converted-audio-chunk notification is fired outside the lock.
+- AC-7: Read-on-stop acquires the lock, copies the entire buffer, releases the lock, and returns the copy.
+- AC-8: Each converted audio chunk is both delivered via the real-time notification and accumulated in the buffer.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:325-345`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0106
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** List input devices with identity and filtering rules
+
+**As a** user selecting an input device
+**I want** the system to enumerate valid input devices from the operating system
+**So that** only usable devices are presented
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: The system enumerates all audio devices from the operating system's hardware service.
+- AC-3: The device list includes only devices with at least one input channel.
+- AC-4: The device list includes only devices with a resolvable human-readable name.
+- AC-5: Devices failing either filtering condition are excluded from the input device list.
+- AC-6: If any hardware query fails, the system returns an empty list.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:347-372`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0107
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Keep audio device management stateless
+
+**As a** consumer of audio device APIs
+**I want** each audio device operation to be treated as an independent query or mutation against the operating system hardware service
+**So that** device management remains stateless
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Audio device management is stateless.
+- AC-2: Each operation is independent of other audio device operations and is performed as a query or mutation against the operating system's audio hardware service.

+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:349-349`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0108
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Get and set the default input device
+
+**As a** system configuring audio input
+**I want** default input device queries and updates to follow the operating system state
+**So that** recording uses the selected device
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Querying the default input device returns the current system default.
+- AC-2: If the default device query fails, querying returns nothing.
+- AC-3: Setting the default input device writes the specified device identifier to the system property.
+- AC-4: Setting the default input device returns a success or failure indicator.

+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:347-372`

+## STORY-0109
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Detect input channels and resolve device names correctly
+
+**As a** device listing logic
+**I want** input-channel detection and name resolution to follow the documented queries
+**So that** device filtering is based on real hardware capabilities
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: To determine whether a device has input channels, the system queries the device's stream configuration in the input scope.
+- AC-2: The system sums the channel count across all audio streams when determining input-channel presence.
+- AC-3: A device has input channels if and only if the total summed channel count is greater than zero.
+- AC-4: To resolve a device name, the system queries the device's name property in the global scope.
+- AC-5: If device name resolution fails, the device is treated as unresolvable and excluded from the input device list.

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Use the configured audio output format
+
+**As a** system

+## STORY-0111

+## STORY-0112
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Apply overlay dismissal and loading durations
+

+## STORY-0113
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Use WAV serialization and overlay size constants

+**So that** file encoding and UI layout stay compatible
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: WAV serialization uses a 44-byte header as the minimum valid WAV file size.
+- AC-2: WAV serialization uses 16 bits per sample with signed integer PCM.
+- AC-3: Float-to-int and int-to-float WAV conversion uses a maximum amplitude scale of 32,767.
+- AC-4: WAV serialize/deserialize round-trips stay within a 0.0001 error tolerance.

+## STORY-0120

+**As a** system

+**So that** captured speech is normalized for transcription
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When recording starts, the audio buffer is cleared, the input format is queried and validated with sample rate greater than 0 and channel count greater than 0, a converter is created to 16kHz mono float32, an audio tap is installed with a roughly 100ms buffer interval, and the audio engine starts.

+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:61-66`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0121
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-004 — Audio Recording Pipeline
+**Title:** Capture audio while the chord is held
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** incoming audio buffers to be converted and accumulated while the push-to-talk keys remain held
+**So that** the spoken utterance is captured for transcription
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: While the chord remains held, audio buffers arrive at roughly 100ms intervals through the input tap callback.
+- AC-2: Each buffer is converted from device format to 16kHz mono float32 and appended to the accumulation buffer under a lock.
+- AC-3: When speaker filtering is active, the audio-chunk callback delivers incremental chunks to the recording session coordinator.

+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:80-83`

+## STORY-0122
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-005 — Dictation Orchestration
+**Title:** Dispatch audio buffers to transcription
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to send Float audio buffers to the speech transcriber under serialized access
+**So that** transcription runs safely and only on valid input
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The pipeline passes the [Float] audio buffer to the speech transcriber.
+- AC-2: Empty buffers are rejected before the serialization queue.
+- AC-3: Access to transcription is serialized with a semaphore and a serial queue.
+- AC-4: The transcriber may return String? and nil is treated as no transcription.
+- AC-5: If a coordinator exists, the recording session manager finalizes diarization and the pipeline uses the filtered transcript when it is non-empty, otherwise it falls through to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:18-22`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:48-50`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0123
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-005 — Dictation Orchestration
+**Title:** Reject Invalid CLI Input
+
+**As a** operator
+**I want** the probe to reject malformed command-line input
+**So that** invalid invocations fail during argument parsing
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Missing values for value-taking flags produce argument parsing errors.
+- AC-2: Omitting `--model` produces an argument parsing error.
+- AC-3: Unknown flags produce argument parsing errors.
+- AC-4: Invalid `--model` values produce argument parsing errors.
+- AC-5: Invalid `--thinking` values produce argument parsing errors.
+- AC-6: Empty or whitespace-only `--input` values produce argument parsing errors.
+- AC-7: The `--input` and `--interactive` combination produces an argument parsing error.
+- AC-8: An unreadable `--window-context-file` produces an argument parsing error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:94-107`
+
+**Status:** pending

+**So that** live recording and archive replay do not run concurrently

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: On successful acquisition, the caller becomes the owner and the pipeline state otherwise remains unchanged on failure.
+- AC-3: If a live recording cannot acquire ownership, the attempt is silently skipped, a debug log entry is written, and the performance trace is abandoned.
+- AC-4: If an experiment archive operation cannot acquire ownership, a `pipeline busy` error is reported to the caller.
+- AC-5: Release by a non-owner is silently ignored.
+- AC-6: The pipeline is released at the end of every session, whether the session succeeds or fails.

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+**So that** FluidAudio results are returned only when meaningful text is produced
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0159
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-005 — Dictation Orchestration
+**Title:** Serialize transcription requests
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** process transcription requests one at a time and handle backend failures safely
+**So that** concurrent requests do not overlap and errors do not leak
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Concurrent transcription requests are serialized so only one runs at a time.
+- AC-2: Empty audio buffers are rejected before entering the serialization gate.
+- AC-3: Work is dispatched onto a serial processing queue.
+- AC-4: Exclusive access is acquired before invoking the backend.
+- AC-5: Exclusive access is released after the backend call completes, regardless of success or failure.
+- AC-6: Backend errors are caught, logged, and returned as no result.
+- AC-7: Transcription failures are never retried.
+- AC-8: The asynchronous result is returned to the caller via a completion callback.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:134-183`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-006 — Cleanup Pipeline

+## STORY-0191

+**Epic:** EPIC-006 — Cleanup Pipeline
+**Title:** Always return a cleanup result
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to request cleaned transcription output that always resolves to a result
+**So that** paste delivery never fails because cleanup was unavailable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Cleanup returns cleaned text, fallback flag, model call duration, post-process duration, and an optional diagnostic transcript.
+- AC-2: If cleanup is unavailable, the pipeline returns deterministic corrections only.
+- AC-3: The pipeline never fails to produce output.
+- AC-4: Transcription completes before cleanup begins, and cleanup completes before paste begins.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:19-24`

+# EPIC-007 — Clipboard Delivery & Fallback
+
+**Summary:** Clipboard Delivery & Fallback

+## STORY-0223
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-007 — Clipboard Delivery & Fallback
+**Title:** Deliver text through the paste system
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to save the clipboard, paste text with Cmd+V, and restore the clipboard afterward
+**So that** transcribed text reaches the target application without losing clipboard state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The paste flow saves the complete clipboard before writing transcribed text.
+- AC-2: The pipeline checks the paste target before simulating Cmd+V.
+- AC-3: The paste flow waits 50 ms before Cmd+V and 100 ms after Cmd+V.
+- AC-4: The clipboard is restored after the post-keystroke delay.
+- AC-5: The only exception is the fallback path, where the transcribed text is intentionally left on the clipboard for manual paste.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:23-24`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:32-50`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0224
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-007 — Clipboard Delivery & Fallback
+**Title:** Return paste result and lifecycle callbacks
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** the paste system to return its result and expose paste lifecycle callbacks
+**So that** callers can observe success, clipboard fallback, and paste session boundaries
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The paste flow returns a paste result indicating success or clipboard fallback.
+- AC-2: The paste system exposes paste start, paste end, and paste session lifecycle callbacks.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:16-16`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0225
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-007 — Clipboard Delivery & Fallback

+
+**As a** hotkey monitor
+**I want** to create the standard Command+V keyDown and keyUp events with the imposed CoreGraphics parameters
+**So that** pasting can be simulated through the system event model
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The event source is `.hidSystemState`.
+- AC-2: The virtual key code is `0x09`.
+- AC-3: The modifier flags are `.maskCommand`.
+- AC-4: The post location is `.cghidEventTap`.
+- AC-5: The simulated paste creates a keyDown and keyUp pair.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:113-123`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-008 — OCR & Screen Context
+
+**Summary:** OCR & Screen Context
+**Stories:** STORY-0260, STORY-0261, STORY-0262, STORY-0263, STORY-0264, STORY-0265, STORY-0266, STORY-0267, STORY-0268, STORY-0269, STORY-0270, STORY-0271, STORY-0272, STORY-0273, STORY-0274, STORY-0275, STORY-0276, STORY-0277, STORY-0278, STORY-0279, STORY-0280, STORY-0281, STORY-0282, STORY-0283, STORY-0284, STORY-0285, STORY-0286, STORY-0287, STORY-0288
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/correction-learning.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md`
+**Status:** 0/29 done
+
+## STORY-0260
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-008 — OCR & Screen Context
+**Title:** Overlap OCR with recording and cleanup prompts
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to start OCR prefetch before recording, resolve it after recording stops, and pass window OCR text into cleanup prompts
+**So that** window context is captured without adding latency to the critical path
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: OCR prefetch starts before recording begins, launches as async background capture at recording start, is resolved after recording stops, and is canceled on error or termination.
+- AC-2: Custom vocabulary words from the correction store are passed at start.
+- AC-3: Window OCR text is truncated to a maximum of 4000 characters, appended as a <WINDOW-OCR-CONTENT> section with <OCR-RULES> instructions, and included only when frontmost window context is enabled and the OCR content is non-nil.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:25-27`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:32-42`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0261
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-008 — OCR & Screen Context
+**Title:** Return OCR prefetch context and timing
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to resolve OCR prefetch with the captured OCR context and elapsed time
+**So that** downstream cleanup can consume the prefetched context and timing information
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Resolving OCR prefetch waits for the async capture job to finish.
+- AC-2: The resolve result includes the OCR context and the elapsed capture time.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:19-19`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0262
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-008 — OCR & Screen Context
+**Title:** Capture and recognize the frontmost window
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** screen capture and OCR to identify the active app, capture its first eligible frontmost window, and recognize its text
+**So that** screen context reflects the actual foreground window
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each capture is stateless and independently identifies the current frontmost window.
+- AC-2: The system queries the operating system workspace for the active application and returns no image when none exists.
+- AC-3: The system selects the first on-screen window whose owner process matches the active application, whose layer is 0, and whose alpha is greater than 0.
+- AC-4: If no matching window is found, the system returns no image.
+- AC-5: ScreenCaptureKit matches the target by platform identifier and captures the window in single-window isolation mode.
+- AC-6: Capture uses 2x frame dimensions, clamps each dimension to a minimum of 1 pixel, and does not scale content.
+- AC-7: The captured image hides the cursor.
+- AC-8: OCR uses Apple Vision accurate mode, language correction, custom vocabulary from preferred transcription terms, and recognition revision 3.
+- AC-9: OCR collects the top candidate from each observation, sorts observations by reading order with the documented vertical-midpoint and same-line horizontal rules, joins candidates with single spaces, computes average confidence, and returns no result when no text is recognized.
+- AC-10: The OCR service always attempts capture regardless of permission preflight status and uses permission status only for diagnostic wording.
+- AC-11: OCR failures return no context and do not crash, throw to callers, or halt the recording pipeline.
+- AC-12: When permission is reported unavailable and an error occurs, the diagnostic message says screen recording permission appears unavailable; otherwise it reports the error without a permission qualifier.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:170-230`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0263
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-008 — OCR & Screen Context
+**Title:** Gate background OCR prefetch and screen context
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** background OCR prefetch and the frontmost-window context feature to obey the documented gating and lifecycle rules
+**So that** prefetch work runs only when it can be used and screen context stays disabled by default
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Background OCR prefetch has the documented Idle, Running, and Resolved states and the documented start, cancel, resolve, and restart transitions.
+- AC-2: The prefetch result includes screen context, which may be absent if capture failed, and the elapsed wall-clock time from capture start to completion.
+- AC-3: Prefetch starts when recording begins only when cleanup is enabled, a cleanup backend is available, and the frontmost window context feature is enabled.
+- AC-4: If any prefetch precondition is not met, any existing prefetch task is cancelled.
+- AC-5: The prefetch uses the user's preferred transcription terms as custom vocabulary.
+- AC-6: When recording stops and window context is enabled, the system resolves the prefetch, uses its context for prompt assembly, and records the prefetch duration in the performance trace.
+- AC-7: The prefetch is cancelled on application state reset, on prepare-for-termination, and if recording start fails.
+- AC-8: The frontmost-window context feature defaults to false.
+- AC-9: When the frontmost-window context feature is disabled, no OCR capture is attempted and no screen content appears in the cleanup prompt.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:232-264`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0289
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Share correction terms between OCR and cleanup
+
+**As a** the correction store
+**I want** to provide the same preferred transcription terms to OCR and deterministic correction
+**So that** preferred terms are both recognized and preserved
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The same preferred transcription terms are passed to the OCR engine as custom words.
+- AC-2: The same terms are also used by the deterministic correction engine.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:26-27`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:54-58`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0290
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Delegate paste target detection and learn from pasted replacements
+
+**As a** the input system
+**I want** to route paste output handling through the accessibility detector and then poll for narrow replacements after paste
+**So that** paste decisions and correction learning use the same AX inspection path
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The paste output handler delegates AX tree inspection to the paste target detector, which uses direct focused element query, cached observation, and recursive AX tree walk with direct query taking priority.
+- AC-2: The coordinator polls focused element text for 15 seconds at 1 second intervals with a 2 second quiescence requirement.
+- AC-3: If a narrow 1-2 word replacement is detected, it is stored as a new misheard replacement in the correction store.
+- AC-4: The post-paste learning flow requires the focused app bundle ID to match the source app.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:27-28`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:40-42`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0291
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Parse Preferred Transcriptions
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** preferred transcriptions to be parsed from a newline-separated draft
+**So that** the app can use a stable ordered list of preferred terms
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The draft is split on newlines.
+- AC-2: Each line is trimmed of whitespace.
+- AC-3: Empty lines are removed.
+- AC-4: Duplicate entries are removed while preserving the order of first occurrence.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md:60-71`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0292
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Parse Commonly Misheard Replacements
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** commonly misheard replacements to be parsed from newline-separated draft text
+**So that** the app can apply ordered wrong-to-right correction pairs
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The draft is split on newlines and empty lines are removed.
+- AC-2: Each line is split on the `->` separator.
+- AC-3: Only lines with exactly one `->` separator are kept.
+- AC-4: Both sides of each kept pair are trimmed of whitespace.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md:60-79`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0293
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Parse preferred transcription drafts
+
+**As a** user

+**So that** I can edit the draft directly while the app keeps a stable structured list
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Missing preferred-transcription drafts default to an empty string.
+- AC-2: The draft is parsed by splitting on newline characters, trimming each line, dropping empty lines, and deduplicating while preserving first-seen order.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:483-557`

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: Commonly misheard replacements are stored under `commonlyMisheardDraft` in UserDefaults.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:489-489`
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:512-512`
