+## STORY-0295
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Expose preferred words to OCR unchanged
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the parsed preferred transcription list to be exposed as a custom word list without transformation
+**So that** OCR can use my preferred words exactly as parsed

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0298

+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning

+**So that** new corrections can be added automatically when appropriate

+- AC-2: The append operation does nothing when an identical replacement already exists in the parsed list.
+- AC-3: A successful append formats the new entry as `wrong -> right`.
+- AC-4: If the current draft does not end with a newline, one newline is inserted before the appended entry.

+## STORY-0299
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Manage correction drafts and deterministic replacement matching
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** correction drafts and deterministic replacements to follow the documented parsing, append, and matching rules
+**So that** stored corrections remain editable while deterministic correction stays predictable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Preferred transcriptions and misheard replacements are stored as raw newline-separated text in user preferences under their documented keys.
+- AC-2: Preferred transcriptions are parsed by splitting on newlines, trimming each line, discarding empty lines, and deduplicating while preserving the first occurrence.
+- AC-3: Misheard replacements are parsed by splitting on newlines, splitting each line on `->`, trimming both sides, discarding lines without exactly two non-empty parts after trimming, and deduplicating.
+- AC-4: Lines without a `->` separator remain in the raw draft text for UI display but are excluded from the parsed replacement list.
+- AC-5: Programmatic append silently ignores empty sides and already-existing replacements, appends formatted `wrong -> right` text with a newline separator when needed, and re-parses the full draft to update both the structured list and persisted text.
+- AC-6: Preferred transcriptions are protected longest-first using case-insensitive word-boundary matching and unique sentinels before replacements run.
+- AC-7: Misheard replacements are applied longest-first by the wrong side using case-insensitive word-boundary matching, regex-escaped phrases, and the documented negative-lookbehind and negative-lookahead rules.
+- AC-8: Empty or whitespace-only phrases produce no match and are skipped.
+- AC-9: Sentinel tokens are restored to their original preferred transcription text after replacements complete.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:272-314`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0300
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-009 — Corrections & Post-Paste Learning
+**Title:** Gate post-paste learning and learned replacement constraints
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** post-paste learning and inferred replacements to obey the documented timing and validation rules
+**So that** new correction rules are learned only from stable, embedded edits
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Post-paste learning is optional and defaults to enabled.
+- AC-2: On paste, when learning is enabled, the first poll is scheduled immediately.
+- AC-3: The documented timing constants are enforced: a 15-second observation window, a 1-second poll interval, a 2-second quiescence period, a maximum replacement word count of 2 words per side, a maximum poll count of 16, and a required stable poll count of 2.
+- AC-4: Each poll reads the current focused element text via the accessibility API and is eligible only while the user remains in the same application where the paste occurred, with window and element frame drift tolerated.
+- AC-5: Learning is checked before scheduling the first poll, during each poll, and before storing a learned correction; if learning becomes disabled at any checkpoint, the operation is abandoned.
+- AC-6: When text is available and no baseline has been captured yet, it becomes the baseline; when text changes the stable count resets; when text stays unchanged the stable count increments; when the stable count reaches 2, replacement inference runs; when the total poll count reaches 16, polling stops.
+- AC-7: Baseline and observed text must be non-empty after trimming and must differ case-insensitively.
+- AC-8: Replacement inference uses a word-level diff with a shared prefix and shared suffix, requires at least one shared prefix or suffix word, and derives the wrong and right sides from the differing middle segment.
+- AC-9: The wrong and right sides must both be non-empty, differ by more than punctuation after normalization, and each be at most 2 words.
+- AC-10: The wrong side must appear as a contiguous word sequence in the originally pasted text.
+- AC-11: When all constraints pass, the replacement is stored through the correction store's programmatic append and a callback notifies the UI layer.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:406-445`
+
+**Status:** pending
+

+**So that** the correction list stays clean

+

+- AC-1: Each edit to the Preferred transcriptions editor is persisted to user defaults.
+- AC-2: The draft is parsed by splitting on newlines, trimming entries, filtering empty lines, and deduplicating the result.

+**Title:** Persist commonly misheard replacements

+# EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+
+**Summary:** Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Stories:** STORY-0349, STORY-0350, STORY-0351, STORY-0352, STORY-0353, STORY-0354, STORY-0355, STORY-0356, STORY-0357, STORY-0358, STORY-0359, STORY-0360, STORY-0361, STORY-0362, STORY-0363, STORY-0364, STORY-0365, STORY-0366, STORY-0367, STORY-0368, STORY-0369, STORY-0370, STORY-0371, STORY-0372, STORY-0373, STORY-0374, STORY-0375, STORY-0376, STORY-0377, STORY-0378, STORY-0379, STORY-0380, STORY-0381, STORY-0382, STORY-0383, STORY-0384, STORY-0385, STORY-0386
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/speech.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/debug-investigation.md`
+**Status:** 0/38 done
+
+## STORY-0349
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Archive and trace sessions
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to archive session data, emit performance traces, and capture sensitive cleanup snapshots under the viewer gate
+**So that** recording sessions remain inspectable without leaking sensitive data
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The archive includes WAV audio plus audio buffer, OCR context, raw transcription, corrected transcription, diarization summary, and all metadata fields, and the store keeps at most 50 entries by pruning the oldest.
+- AC-2: A trace is created on hotkey start, records 14 milestone timestamps or durations, completes after paste end, and writes a Performance debug log entry unless the pipeline is busy or recording start fails.
+- AC-3: Raw transcription, cleanup config, window context, and cleaned output are captured as a Cleanup log entry only when the debug log viewer count is greater than 0, and the entry is persisted to disk.
+- AC-4: Archive failure is logged but does not interrupt the pipeline.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:28-30`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:43-44`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0350
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Log model and diarization failures
+
+**As a** operator
+**I want** key model and diarization events to be logged under the model category
+**So that** I can trace loading and failure behavior
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Model loading initiated is logged under the model category with the model name.
+- AC-2: Model loading succeeded is logged under the model category with the model name.
+- AC-3: Model loading timed out and retrying is logged under the model category.
+- AC-4: Model loading failed is logged under the model category with an error description.
+- AC-5: Transcription failed is logged under the model category with the model name and error description.
+- AC-6: Diarization chunk processing failed is logged under the model category with an error description.
+- AC-7: Diarization model loading failed is logged under the model category with an error description.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:432-446`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0351
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Persist debug log entries safely
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** debug log entries to be recorded, bounded, and recovered from disk safely
+**So that** the log remains observable without corrupting the store
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The store persists log entries to `<Application Support>/GhostPepper/debug-log.json` by default.
+- AC-2: The persisted file is a JSON array of entry objects with `id`, `timestamp`, `category`, and `message` fields.
+- AC-3: Recording an entry persists the complete entry array to disk immediately.
+- AC-4: Clearing the store persists an empty entry array to disk immediately.
+- AC-5: Writes are synchronous on the calling thread.
+- AC-6: Writes are atomic by writing a temporary file and renaming it into place.
+- AC-7: When the entry count exceeds 250, the oldest entries are removed until the count is 250.
+- AC-8: Capacity trimming occurs after each record and during initialization after loading persisted entries.
+- AC-9: Missing, unreadable, or invalid JSON files cause the store to start with an empty entry list without surfacing an error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:273-378`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0352
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Gate sensitive debug logs by viewer count
+
+**As a** privacy-conscious user
+**I want** sensitive debug entries to be recorded only when a viewer is present
+**So that** private log data is not stored unless the log is actively being viewed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Sensitive-record operations discard the entry when the viewer count is zero.
+- AC-2: Sensitive-record operations record the entry normally when the viewer count is greater than zero.
+- AC-3: Incrementing the viewer count always increases it by exactly one.
+- AC-4: Decrementing the viewer count reduces it by one but never below zero.
+- AC-5: Sensitive entries that pass the viewer gate are persisted to disk.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:273-378`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0353
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Format debug log output text
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the debug log store to render a textual view of entries
+**So that** the log can be displayed in a human-readable form
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The formatted output renders each entry as `[HH:mm:ss] [Category] message`.
+- AC-2: Entries in the formatted output are separated by double newlines.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:273-378`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0354
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Keep debug log entries in memory
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** debug log entries to remain resident in memory with the viewer gate starting closed
+**So that** the UI can observe the current log state without depending only on disk reads
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Debug log entries are held in memory for UI observation.
+- AC-2: The viewer count is initialized to zero.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:302-302`
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:321-321`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0355
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Define debug log entry schema and configurable cap
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** debug log entries to preserve their documented field types and limit configuration
+**So that** the store remains schema-correct and bounded by the configured capacity
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `id` is encoded as a UUID string.
+- AC-2: `timestamp` is encoded as a Date number.
+- AC-3: `category` is limited to `Hotkey`, `OCR`, `Cleanup`, `Model`, or `Performance`.
+- AC-4: `message` is encoded as a string.
+- AC-5: The debug-log entry cap is configurable and defaults to 250 entries.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:306-315`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0356
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Keep debug log storage overridable and silent on I/O failure
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** debug log storage to support test overrides and fail quietly on file I/O problems
+**So that** tests can redirect storage and users are not exposed to persistence errors
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The debug-log storage file is overridable for testing.
+- AC-2: All debug-log file I/O errors are swallowed silently.
+- AC-3: Failed writes do not retry and do not surface a notification.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:304-304`
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:327-327`

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each log entry contains a unique auto-generated identifier, a creation timestamp, a log category, and a free-form message.
+- AC-2: Log entries support JSON serialization for disk persistence.
+- AC-3: Log entries support equality comparison for use in reactive UI collections.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:428-493`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0368
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Initialize the log store with capacity and persisted data
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the log store to load persisted entries and enforce its capacity on startup
+**So that** log history survives restarts without exceeding the limit
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The log store is a single-instance observable object that publishes entry-list changes.
+- AC-2: The store defaults to a maximum of 250 entries unless a different maximum is supplied.
+- AC-3: The store loads previously persisted entries from the default file path `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/debug-log.json` unless another storage location is supplied.
+- AC-4: If the storage file does not exist, is unreadable, or contains invalid data, the store starts with an empty list silently.
+- AC-5: The store trims entries to the configured capacity during initialization.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:428-493`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0369
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Record and clear log entries with persistence
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** record and clear operations to update the log list and persist it
+**So that** operational events are durable on disk
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Recording appends a new entry with the current time, specified category, and specified message.
+- AC-2: Recording trims the log to capacity after append and persists all entries to disk.
+- AC-3: Clearing removes all entries and immediately persists the empty state to disk.
+- AC-4: Recording is unconditional and always succeeds.
+- AC-5: The complete entry list is persisted as a JSON array on every record and clear operation.
+- AC-6: Parent directories are created before writing if they do not exist.
+- AC-7: File writes use a temporary file and rename sequence.
+- AC-8: All file I/O is synchronous and I/O errors are silently suppressed.
+- AC-9: Entries survive application restarts via the persisted file.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:428-493`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0370
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Gate sensitive cleanup logging on the live viewer count
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** sensitive records to depend on the debug viewer's live count
+**So that** cleanup content is only captured while a viewer is open
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The live viewer count starts at zero.
+- AC-2: When the count is zero, sensitive recording requests are discarded without creating an entry or writing to disk.
+- AC-3: When the count is greater than zero, sensitive recording requests are handled like standard recording requests.
+- AC-4: The count increments when a viewer begins live viewing and decrements when it ends.
+- AC-5: The decrement is clamped so the count never goes below zero.
+- AC-6: Multiple concurrent viewers are supported by the counter.
+- AC-7: The sensitive gate applies to raw transcription text, cleanup configuration details, window context summaries, and final cleaned output text, all under the Cleanup category.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:428-493`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0371
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Preserve sensitive log entries after the debug viewer closes
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** sensitive Cleanup entries already recorded while the viewer was open to remain intact after closure
+**So that** closing the viewer does not purge or redact previously captured content
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Sensitive Cleanup entries recorded while the debug viewer is open remain on disk until they age out of the 250-entry capacity limit or are manually cleared.
+- AC-2: Closing the debug viewer does not automatically expire, scrub, or redact sensitive entries.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:489-491`

+**Status:** pending

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: Entries are separated by double newlines.

+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:428-493`

+**Status:** pending

+**Title:** Open the debug log viewer

+**As a** debugging user

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Limit the viewer to 250 entries

+**As a** debugging user

+## STORY-0377

+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Record sensitive entries while viewer is open
+
+**As a** debugging user
+**I want** sensitive dictation details to be captured only when the viewer is open
+**So that** I can inspect full diagnostics during an investigation
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the live viewer count is greater than 0, raw transcription text, cleanup configuration details, window context summary, and final cleaned output are captured.
+- AC-2: When the live viewer count is 0, sensitive recording requests are silently discarded.

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Persist logs on every record operation

+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: All entries, sensitive or non-sensitive, are persisted to the debug log file on every record operation.

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics
+**Title:** Close the debug log viewer

+**As a** debugging user

+## STORY-0382
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-010 — Debug Logging & Diagnostics

+# EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+
+**Summary:** Overlay Feedback
+**Stories:** STORY-0387, STORY-0388, STORY-0389, STORY-0390, STORY-0391, STORY-0392, STORY-0393, STORY-0394, STORY-0395, STORY-0396, STORY-0397, STORY-0398, STORY-0399, STORY-0400, STORY-0401, STORY-0402, STORY-0403, STORY-0404, STORY-0405, STORY-0406
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md`
+**Status:** 0/20 done
+
+## STORY-0387
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Show overlay messages and play sounds
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to update overlay messages through the session and play system sounds when enabled
+**So that** the user gets stateful audio and visual feedback
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The overlay message sequence includes Recording, Transcribing, Cleaning Up, and dismissed, with conditional messages for No Sound Detected, Clipboard Fallback, and Learned Correction.
+- AC-2: Each new overlay message replaces the current one and cancels pending auto-dismiss timers.
+- AC-3: The start sound is Tink and the stop sound is Pop, both via NSSound, and playback is gated by playSounds.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:29-30`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:52-53`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0388
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Show no-sound feedback for long empty transcriptions
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a clear signal when a long recording contains no sound
+**So that** I can tell silence from a failed transcription
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the audio buffer has 80,000 or more samples and transcription returns nothing, the overlay shows `No sound detected` with an orange indicator.
+- AC-2: The `No sound detected` overlay auto-dismisses after 3 seconds.
+- AC-3: The `No sound detected` overlay shows no secondary text.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:177-197`
+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:216-217`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0389
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Show exact session overlay states and dismissal
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the session overlay to use the exact documented copy and indicator styling
+**So that** recording state is communicated consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The recording overlay shows `Recording...` with a pulsing red indicator.
+- AC-2: The transcribing overlay shows `Transcribing...` with a blue indicator.
+- AC-3: The cleaning up overlay shows `Cleaning up...` with a blue indicator.
+- AC-4: The floating overlay is dismissed at the end of the session.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:13-56`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0390
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Load the start and stop sound effects
+
+**As a** user of recording feedback sounds
+**I want** the system to load the documented sounds at initialization
+**So that** recording events can trigger the correct effects
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The system loads the start-recording sound using the system sound "Tink" at initialization.
+- AC-2: The system loads the stop-recording sound using the system sound "Pop" at initialization.
+- AC-3: If a named system sound does not exist on the platform, the reference is absent.
+- AC-4: If a sound reference is absent, playback silently does nothing.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:427-449`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0391
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Gate playback with enable/disable state
+
+**As a** system issuing sound effects
+**I want** every playback request to check an enable/disable gate first
+**So that** sound effects can be suppressed when disabled
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Every playback request checks the enable/disable gate before playing.
+- AC-2: If the gate returns disabled, the playback request is silently ignored.
+- AC-3: If no custom gate is provided, the gate defaults to enabled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:427-449`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0392
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Stop any currently playing sound before replaying it
+
+**As a** listener triggering sound effects repeatedly
+**I want** a sound effect to stop its current instance before starting again
+**So that** rapid retriggering works without overlap
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When playing a sound effect, any currently playing instance of that sound is stopped before playback starts.
+- AC-2: Rapid retriggering can start a new playback without waiting for the previous instance to finish.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:427-449`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0393
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Allow dependency injection for sound-effect testing
+
+**As a** tester
+**I want** custom sound gate and player implementations to be injectable
+**So that** sound-effect behavior can be tested without actual audio playback
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The system accepts an optional custom enable/disable gate implementation.
+- AC-2: The system accepts an optional custom start-recording player implementation.
+- AC-3: The system accepts an optional custom stop-recording player implementation.
+- AC-4: These injection points allow testing without actual audio playback.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:427-449`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0394
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Render overlay message catalog
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** each overlay message to use the specified text, indicator, size, and dismissal behavior
+**So that** pipeline status feedback is consistent and predictable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When recording is active, the overlay shows `Recording...` with no secondary text, a pulsing red dot, size 300x60, and no auto-dismiss timer.
+- AC-2: During model loading, the overlay shows `Loading models...` with no secondary text, an orange spinner, size 300x60, and no auto-dismiss timer.
+- AC-3: During transcription, the overlay shows `Transcribing...` with no secondary text, a pulsing blue dot, size 300x60, and no auto-dismiss timer.
+- AC-4: During cleanup, the overlay shows `Cleaning up...` with no secondary text, a pulsing blue dot, size 300x60, and no auto-dismiss timer.
+- AC-5: When no sound is detected, the overlay shows `No sound detected` with no secondary text, a pulsing orange dot, size 300x60, and a 3-second auto-dismiss.
+- AC-6: When clipboard fallback occurs, the overlay shows `Copied to clipboard` with secondary text `Cmd+V to paste`, a pulsing blue dot, size 420x84, and a 3-second auto-dismiss.
+- AC-7: When a learned correction is shown, the overlay shows `Learned correction` with secondary text in the form `<wrong> -> <right>`, a green checkmark, size 420x84, and a 3-second auto-dismiss.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:451-485`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0395
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Advance overlay through a successful session
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the overlay to follow the successful recording flow
+**So that** I can see the session progress clearly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When recording starts, `Recording...` is shown.
+- AC-2: When recording stops and transcription begins, `Transcribing...` replaces the recording message.
+- AC-3: When text refinement begins and refinement is available, `Cleaning up...` replaces the transcribing message.
+- AC-4: After successful text delivery, the overlay is dismissed.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:451-485`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0396
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Skip cleanup when refinement is unavailable
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the overlay to move directly from transcription to dismissal when refinement is unavailable
+**So that** the user is not shown a cleanup state that cannot occur
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If text refinement is unavailable, the overlay transitions directly from `Transcribing...` to dismissed.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:451-485`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0397
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Handle clipboard fallback and empty transcript overlay states
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** fallback and empty-transcript cases to use the correct overlay behavior
+**So that** I understand why delivery changed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If delivery falls back to the clipboard, the `Copied to clipboard` overlay is shown.
+- AC-2: When clipboard fallback is shown, the pipeline schedules its own 2.5-second dismiss timer in addition to the overlay's built-in 3-second auto-dismiss timer.
+- AC-3: If no transcript is produced for a short recording that was cancelled, the overlay is dismissed immediately.
+- AC-4: If no transcript is produced for a long recording with no speech, the overlay is replaced with `No Sound Detected`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:451-485`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0398
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Replace overlay messages atomically
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** new overlay messages to fully replace the current one
+**So that** timers, size, and content stay in sync
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Showing a new message replaces the currently displayed message.
+- AC-2: When a new message is shown, any pending auto-dismiss timer for the previous message is cancelled.
+- AC-3: When a new message requires a different size, the panel resizes to match the new message.
+- AC-4: When the new message type requires auto-dismiss, a new auto-dismiss timer is scheduled for that message.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:451-485`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0399
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Ignore mismatched conditional dismisses
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** conditional dismiss requests to only apply to the currently shown overlay message
+**So that** a stale dismiss does not close the wrong message
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A conditional dismiss only dismisses the overlay when the currently displayed message matches the specified message.
+- AC-2: If a different message is showing, the conditional dismiss request is ignored.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:451-485`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0400
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Display overlay message text
+
+**As a** person using Ghost Pepper
+**I want** the recording overlay to show the correct text for each pipeline state

+

+- AC-1: The Recording message shows primary text "Recording..." and no secondary text.

+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:226-274`

+## STORY-0401
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Present the recording overlay as a floating indicator
+
+**As a** person using Ghost Pepper
+**I want** the overlay to be a non-interactive floating panel with the specified placement

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0402
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Size overlay messages appropriately
+
+**As a** person using Ghost Pepper
+**I want** overlay sizes to match the message type
+**So that** messages remain visually balanced
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Clipboard fallback and learned correction messages are 420 x 84 points.
+- AC-2: All other messages are 300 x 60 points.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:226-274`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0403
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Update overlay messages in place
+
+**As a** person using Ghost Pepper
+**I want** overlay updates and dismissals to reuse the existing panel and manage timers correctly
+**So that** status messages behave predictably
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Showing a message when a panel already exists updates the existing panel rather than creating a new one.
+- AC-2: Showing a message always cancels any pending auto-dismiss timer first.
+- AC-3: Clipboard fallback, learned correction, and no-sound-detected messages auto-dismiss after 3 seconds.
+- AC-4: All other messages persist until explicitly dismissed.
+- AC-5: Dismissing the overlay cancels any pending timer, hides the panel, and releases all references.
+- AC-6: Conditional dismiss only dismisses when the currently displayed message matches the specified one; otherwise it does nothing.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:226-274`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0404
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-011 — Overlay Feedback
+**Title:** Render overlay visuals by state
+
+**As a** person using Ghost Pepper
+**I want** the overlay to use the specified visual treatments for each message type
+**So that** status is instantly recognizable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The overlay uses a capsule-shaped background filled with black at 85% opacity.
+- AC-2: Recording uses a red dot with pulsing animation.
+- AC-3: Model loading uses a progress spinner and no dot.
+- AC-4: Cleaning up, transcribing, and clipboard fallback use a blue dot with pulsing animation.
+- AC-5: No sound detected uses an orange dot with pulsing animation.
+- AC-6: Learned correction uses a green checkmark circle icon with no dot and no pulse.
+- AC-7: Pulsing animation oscillates opacity between 0.4 and 1.0, uses an ease-in-out curve, lasts 0.6 seconds, repeats forever, and autoreverses.
+- AC-8: Primary text is white, 13pt semibold.
+- AC-9: Secondary text is white at 80% opacity, 12pt medium weight with rounded design, and limited to 2 lines.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:226-274`
+
+**Status:** pending

+

+**Title:** Show recording start feedback

+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:71-73`

+# EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+
+**Summary:** Transcription Lab
+**Stories:** STORY-0407, STORY-0408, STORY-0409, STORY-0410, STORY-0411, STORY-0412, STORY-0413, STORY-0414, STORY-0415, STORY-0416, STORY-0417, STORY-0418, STORY-0419, STORY-0420, STORY-0421, STORY-0422, STORY-0423, STORY-0424, STORY-0425, STORY-0426, STORY-0427, STORY-0428, STORY-0429, STORY-0430, STORY-0431, STORY-0432, STORY-0433, STORY-0434, STORY-0435, STORY-0436, STORY-0437, STORY-0438, STORY-0439, STORY-0440, STORY-0441, STORY-0442, STORY-0443, STORY-0444, STORY-0445, STORY-0446, STORY-0447, STORY-0448, STORY-0449
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/transcription-lab.md`
+**Status:** 0/43 done
+
+## STORY-0407
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Enforce exclusive pipeline ownership and lab access
+
+**As a** the core pipeline
+**I want** to allow only one live recording or lab consumer at a time and make lab runs use the same mutex
+**So that** audio capture and lab experiments never overlap
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The pipeline owner mutex permits only one owner at a time, the two owners are live recording and the transcription lab, acquire returns a boolean, and release succeeds only if the caller is the current owner.
+- AC-2: Live recording is silently skipped when the pipeline is busy and the lab throws a pipeline busy error when it cannot acquire the mutex.
+- AC-3: Both live recording and lab experiments acquire the pipeline before operating and release it in a deferred block.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:21-22`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:46-48`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0408
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Reject busy pipeline access
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** reruns to fail when the pipeline is already owned
+**So that** transcription and cleanup do not overlap
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The acquisition closure must succeed before a rerun proceeds.
+- AC-2: If acquisition returns false, the rerun throws a `pipeline busy` error.
+- AC-3: The release closure runs on exit whether the operation succeeds or fails.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0409
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Re-run transcription from archived audio
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the experiment runner to replay transcription against saved audio
+**So that** I can compare models on the same recording
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The transcription rerun acquires the pipeline lock, loads the archived audio, loads the requested speech model, and transcribes the audio in that order.
+- AC-2: If the archived audio buffer is empty, the rerun throws `missing audio`.
+- AC-3: If transcription returns nil, the rerun throws `transcription failed`.
+- AC-4: On success, the rerun returns the raw transcription text.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0410
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Re-run cleanup from archived audio
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the experiment runner to replay cleanup against saved audio
+**So that** I can compare cleanup models on the same transcript
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The cleanup rerun acquires the pipeline lock, builds the full prompt, runs cleanup with the raw transcription and selected cleanup model, and returns the corrected text, fallback indicator, and diagnostic transcript.
+- AC-2: The prompt is built from the base prompt, archived screen context, preferred transcriptions, commonly misheard words, and the screen context toggle.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0411
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Capture diagnostic cleanup transcripts
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** cleanup reruns to emit diagnostics
+**So that** I can inspect what the model saw and produced
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The diagnostic transcript includes the full prompt sent to the model.
+- AC-2: The diagnostic transcript includes the input text actually received by the model, including deterministic pre-cleanup corrections.
+- AC-3: The diagnostic transcript includes the raw model output when one exists.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0412
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Publish history panel state
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the history panel to expose the documented state fields
+**So that** I can select entries and run experiments consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The panel publishes a read-only entry list in newest-first order.
+- AC-2: The panel publishes selected entry identifier, selected speech model identifier, selected cleanup model kind, and screen context toggle as writable state.
+- AC-3: The panel publishes experiment raw transcription, corrected transcription, transcription duration, cleanup duration, latest cleanup transcript, running stage, and optional error message as read-only state.
+- AC-4: Changing the selected speech model or cleanup model is observable through the published state.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0413
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Select history entries safely

+**As a** transcription lab user

+**So that** the detail view stays consistent with the selected recording
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Selecting an entry verifies that the entry exists before updating selection.
+- AC-2: Selecting an entry sets the selected identifier and sets the screen context toggle based on whether the entry has screen context.

+

+**So that** I can still inspect the original recording text

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: The displayed corrected transcription uses the rerun result when it is non-empty, otherwise it uses the selected entry's corrected transcription.

+## STORY-0416
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Orchestrate transcription reruns
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** transcription reruns to follow the documented state transitions and error mapping
+**So that** I get consistent feedback from the history panel
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If no entry is selected, transcription rerun returns `Choose a saved recording first.`
+- AC-2: Before calling the transcription runner, the UI sets the running stage to transcription, clears the error, and clears durations.
+- AC-3: On success, the UI stores the raw transcription result and clears corrected transcription and cleanup duration as stale.
+- AC-4: Pipeline busy maps to `Ghost Pepper is busy with another recording or lab run.`
+- AC-5: Missing audio maps to `This saved recording no longer has playable audio.`
+- AC-6: Transcription failed maps to `That model could not produce a transcription for this recording.`

+- AC-8: The running stage returns to idle after the rerun finishes.

+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`

+## STORY-0417
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Orchestrate cleanup reruns
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** cleanup reruns to follow the documented state transitions and error mapping
+**So that** I get consistent feedback from the history panel
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If no entry is selected, the cleanup rerun validates that state before proceeding.
+- AC-2: If the raw transcription input is empty, the UI errors with `Run transcription first or choose a recording with a raw transcription.`
+- AC-3: Before calling the cleanup runner, the UI sets the running stage to cleanup and clears cleanup-specific state.
+- AC-4: On success, the UI stores corrected text, duration, and diagnostic transcript.
+- AC-5: Pipeline busy maps to `Ghost Pepper is busy with another recording or lab run.`
+- AC-6: Missing audio maps to `This saved recording no longer has playable audio.`
+- AC-7: Transcription failed maps to `That model could not produce a transcription for this recording.`
+- AC-8: Other cleanup errors map to `The cleanup rerun failed.`
+- AC-9: The running stage returns to idle after the rerun finishes.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0418
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Reload history state from disk

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Changing the selected speech model pushes the change back to the broader application state.
+- AC-2: Changing the selected cleanup model kind pushes the change back to the broader application state.
+- AC-3: A batch update operation can set both models without firing synchronizer callbacks.
+- AC-4: The batch update path is used during programmatic initialization.

+## STORY-0420
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Share the pipeline mutex between live recording and lab runs

+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** live recording and transcription lab work to share one mutex
+**So that** only one owner can use the pipeline at a time
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The pipeline has two owners only: live recording and transcription lab.
+- AC-2: Acquisition succeeds and sets the owner only when no other owner currently holds the pipeline.
+- AC-3: Acquisition returns false when another owner already holds the pipeline.
+- AC-4: Release clears the owner only when the releasing party matches the current owner.

+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Lock reruns at the application layer
+
+**As a** transcription lab user

+**So that** the runner itself can stay agnostic about global locking
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The application acquires the pipeline lock before calling the experiment runner.
+- AC-2: The runner receives no-op acquisition and release closures that always succeed.
+- AC-3: The actual locking occurs at the application layer.
+- AC-4: The pipeline is released on both success and failure paths.

+## STORY-0424

+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab
+**Title:** Refine single-word replacements at the character level
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** single-word replace pairs to be refined character-by-character when useful
+**So that** partial overlaps are shown more precisely

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0437
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab

+

+- AC-3: The active raw transcription comes from the rerun result when non-empty, otherwise from the entry's original raw transcription.
+- AC-4: Starting cleanup sets the running stage to cleanup, clears cleanup-specific state, acquires the pipeline, and builds the full cleanup prompt from the base prompt, optional archived screen context, preferred transcriptions, and commonly misheard words.
+- AC-5: On success, the corrected transcription, cleanup duration, and diagnostic transcript are stored, the pipeline is released, and the running stage returns to nil.

+- `specs/specs/journeys/transcription-lab.md:153-178`

+

+**Title:** Open the full cleanup transcript
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** to inspect the complete cleanup prompt and raw model output
+**So that** I can audit what the model actually saw
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Clicking "Show full cleanup transcript" opens a separate window.

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+**Status:** pending

+

+**Title:** Keep lab experiments non-destructive

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+## STORY-0442

+**Epic:** EPIC-012 — Transcription Lab

+# EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+
+**Summary:** Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Stories:** STORY-0450, STORY-0451, STORY-0452, STORY-0453, STORY-0454, STORY-0455, STORY-0456, STORY-0457, STORY-0458, STORY-0459, STORY-0460, STORY-0461, STORY-0462, STORY-0463, STORY-0464, STORY-0465, STORY-0466, STORY-0467, STORY-0468, STORY-0469, STORY-0470
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`
+**Status:** 0/21 done
+
+## STORY-0450
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Check for updates through Sparkle
+
+**As a** the distribution layer
+**I want** to trigger Sparkle update checks from the menu and on the 24-hour schedule
+**So that** users can update the app and the updater verifies signatures
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The Check for Updates menu item triggers a Sparkle update check.
+- AC-2: Sparkle is lazily initialized.
+- AC-3: Auto-check runs every 24 hours.
+- AC-4: Update verification uses EdDSA with the embedded public key.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/behavioral-integration.md:30-30`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0451
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Declare only the microphone input entitlement
+
+**As a** app bundle
+**I want** to declare exactly one entitlement for audio input
+**So that** the app can access the microphone and nothing else is added at the entitlement layer
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app declares `com.apple.security.device.audio-input` with value `true`.
+- AC-2: No other entitlements are declared.
+- AC-3: The app sandbox remains disabled (`ENABLE_APP_SANDBOX: NO`).
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:57-64`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0452
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Preapprove Accessibility for managed devices
+
+**As a** administrator
+**I want** to pre-approve Accessibility permissions for the app in MDM
+**So that** managed deployments can avoid the normal Accessibility approval flow
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The pre-approval bundle ID is `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper`.
+- AC-2: The pre-approval team ID is `BBVMGXR9AY`.
+- AC-3: The only pre-approvable permission in this contract is Accessibility.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:66-72`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0453
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Target the required deployment platform
+
+**As a** release process
+**I want** to enforce the minimum supported macOS version and architecture
+**So that** the app only ships to supported hardware and operating systems
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The minimum supported macOS version is 14.0 (Sonoma).
+- AC-2: The required architecture is Apple Silicon (`arm64`).
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:88-96`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0454
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Document first-launch resource expectations
+
+**As a** release process
+**I want** to surface the approximate RAM and disk needs for model downloads
+**So that** users understand the footprint before installing or launching
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The documented approximate RAM requirement is about 2 GB for speech and cleanup models.
+- AC-2: The documented approximate disk requirement is about 1.5 GB for model downloads on first launch.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:88-104`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0455
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Relaunch through a new app instance
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to relaunch by spawning a new instance and then terminating the current one
+**So that** it can restart after permission changes or error recovery
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The relaunch operation executes `/usr/bin/open` with `-n` and the current bundle URL path.
+- AC-2: The current application terminates only after the new instance has been spawned.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:612-622`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0456
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Show relaunch spawn failures
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** spawn failures during relaunch to surface as an application error message
+**So that** I can see why restart failed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A relaunch spawn failure is surfaced as `Failed to relaunch Ghost Pepper: [error description]`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:612-622`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0457
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Disable the sandbox
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the app sandbox to be disabled
+**So that** global keyboard taps, simulated keystrokes, and screen capture remain possible
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The build configuration has the application sandbox disabled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:624-636`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0458
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Disable hardened runtime in build config
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the hardened runtime to be disabled in the build configuration
+**So that** archive or export time can override it for distribution builds
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The build configuration disables the hardened runtime.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:624-636`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0459
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Override hardened runtime for distribution builds
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** distribution archive or export steps to override the disabled hardened runtime
+**So that** notarization can succeed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Distribution archive or export steps override the hardened runtime setting.
+- AC-2: The override exists so notarization can succeed.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:628-628`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0460
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Sign with Apple Development credentials
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** automatic code signing to use the Apple Development identity and team `BBVMGXR9AY`
+**So that** development builds are signed consistently and entitlements can be adjusted during signing
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Automatic code signing uses the `Apple Development` identity.
+- AC-2: The development team identifier is `BBVMGXR9AY`.
+- AC-3: Entitlements modification is permitted during signing.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:624-636`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0461
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Declare only audio input entitlement
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the app to declare only the audio input device entitlement
+**So that** the entitlement surface stays minimal
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The only declared entitlement is `com.apple.security.device.audio-input`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:624-636`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0462
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Run as a menu-bar app
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to declare itself as a UI element
+**So that** it runs without a Dock icon during normal operation
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `LSUIElement` is set to `true`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:624-636`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0463
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Automate DMG packaging in order
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** the build script to package the release DMG through the documented sequence
+**So that** release artifacts are produced consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The script reads the version and build number from Info.plist before building.
+- AC-2: The script cleans prior build artifacts, builds a release configuration with `-skipMacroValidation`, verifies the app bundle exists, copies it to staging with an `/Applications` symlink, creates a UDZO DMG, signs the DMG if `sign_update` is available, cleans staging and derived data, and prints the DMG path, size, and next steps.
+- AC-3: If the Sparkle `sign_update` tool is not found, the script prints a warning instead of failing.
+- AC-4: The script does not perform notarization or additional code signing beyond what Xcode applies during the build.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0464
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Use the project name as the DMG volume name
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** the packaged DMG to use the project name as its volume name
+**So that** the release image is named as specified
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The DMG volume name is the project name.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:680-680`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0465
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Publish the DMG at the release URL
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** the DMG to be hosted at the documented GitHub release asset URL pattern
+**So that** release links are predictable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The DMG is published at `https://github.com/matthartman/ghost-pepper/releases/download/v{version}/GhostPepper.dmg`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0466
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Require macOS 14 or later
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to require macOS 14.0 or later
+**So that** deployment matches the supported system version
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The deployment target and the appcast minimum system version both require macOS 14.0 or later.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:716-724`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0467
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Require Apple Silicon hardware
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the app to require Apple Silicon hardware
+**So that** on-device ML inference works with the documented dependencies
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The application is documented as requiring M1 or later hardware.
+- AC-2: The requirement is derived from WhisperKit and FluidAudio rather than an explicit architecture constraint in project configuration.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:716-724`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0468
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Document first-launch resource needs
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the release notes to state the app's approximate RAM and disk needs
+**So that** I know the expected first-launch footprint
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The release notes state approximately 2 GB of RAM is required for both speech and cleanup models.
+- AC-2: The release notes state approximately 1.5 GB of disk space is required for model downloads on first launch.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:716-724`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0469
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Support accessibility PPPC pre-approval
+
+**As a** IT administrator
+**I want** to pre-approve accessibility permission with a PPPC profile
+**So that** managed devices can grant the app accessibility access ahead of time
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A PPPC profile can reference bundle identifier `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper`, team identifier `BBVMGXR9AY`, and the Accessibility permission for pre-approval.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:726-738`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0470
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-013 — Deployment & Enterprise Support
+**Title:** Keep MDM behavior passive
+
+**As a** IT administrator
+**I want** the app to have no MDM-specific logic
+**So that** system-level pre-approval works without app-side enterprise code
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: The app only benefits passively from macOS-level permission pre-approval.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:726-738`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-014 — Cleanup Probe CLI
+
+**Summary:** Cleanup Probe CLI
+**Stories:** STORY-0471, STORY-0472, STORY-0473, STORY-0474, STORY-0475, STORY-0476, STORY-0477, STORY-0478, STORY-0479, STORY-0480, STORY-0481, STORY-0482, STORY-0483, STORY-0484, STORY-0485, STORY-0486, STORY-0487, STORY-0488, STORY-0489, STORY-0490, STORY-0491, STORY-0492, STORY-0493
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`
+**Status:** 0/23 done
+
+## STORY-0471
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-014 — Cleanup Probe CLI
+**Title:** Launch Through Wrapper
+
+**As a** shell user
+**I want** to run the cleanup model probe through its wrapper script
+**So that** the tool builds itself if needed and forwards my arguments unchanged
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The probe is exposed as a standalone command-line executable separate from the main application target.
+- AC-2: The wrapper script builds the tool if necessary before execution.
+- AC-3: The wrapper script forwards all provided arguments to the probe.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:8-10`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0472
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-014 — Cleanup Probe CLI
+**Title:** Select Cleanup Model
+
+**As a** operator
+**I want** to choose the cleanup model with `--model`
+**So that** the probe loads the requested inference model
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `--model` is required on the command line.
+- AC-2: `--model` accepts `fast`, `full`, or a recognized model identifier.
+- AC-3: The chosen value determines which cleanup model is loaded for inference.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:14-18`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0473
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-014 — Cleanup Probe CLI
+**Title:** Run One-Shot Cleanup
+
+**As a** operator
+**I want** to pass a single input string with `--input`
+**So that** the probe cleans one text without entering REPL mode
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `--input` accepts a text string.
+- AC-2: Supplying `--input` runs the tool in one-shot mode.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:14-19`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:38-42`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0474
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-014 — Cleanup Probe CLI
+**Title:** Override Cleanup Prompt
+
+**As a** operator
+**I want** to provide a custom system prompt with `--prompt`
+**So that** the LLM uses my prompt instead of the built-in default
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `--prompt` accepts a text string.
+- AC-2: When `--prompt` is provided, the probe uses that prompt as the system prompt.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:14-20`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:78-82`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0475
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-014 — Cleanup Probe CLI
+**Title:** Supply Inline Window Context
+
+**As a** operator
+**I want** to pass OCR window context inline with `--window-context`
+**So that** the probe can use the provided context text
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `--window-context` accepts a text string.
+- AC-2: The provided text is treated as inline OCR window context.

+# EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+
+**Summary:** Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Stories:** STORY-0539, STORY-0540, STORY-0541, STORY-0542, STORY-0543, STORY-0544, STORY-0545, STORY-0546, STORY-0547, STORY-0548, STORY-0549, STORY-0550, STORY-0551, STORY-0552, STORY-0553, STORY-0554, STORY-0555
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md`, `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md`
+**Status:** 0/17 done
+
+## STORY-0539
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Open Main App Preferences Domain
+
+**As a** runtime component
+**I want** to read the main application's preferences from the hardcoded suite name
+**So that** the probe can mirror the user's configured cleanup behavior
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The probe opens UserDefaults using the hardcoded suite name `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper`.
+- AC-2: If the suite cannot be opened, the probe falls back to the standard UserDefaults domain.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:70-72`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:86-86`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0540
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Respect Prompt Preference Precedence
+
+**As a** runtime component
+**I want** the cleanup prompt to come from `cleanupPrompt` unless `--prompt` is provided
+**So that** CLI input overrides the stored prompt only when explicitly requested
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When `--prompt` is not provided, the probe uses the `cleanupPrompt` preference value.
+- AC-2: When `--prompt` is provided, it takes unconditional precedence over the stored `cleanupPrompt` preference value.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:76-82`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0541
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Read Correction Preferences From Main App
+
+**As a** runtime component
+**I want** the probe to read correction store keys from the main application's preferences
+**So that** it can reproduce the same deterministic corrections as the main app
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `preferredTranscriptionsDraft` is read from the main application's preferences and has no CLI override.
+- AC-2: `commonlyMisheardDraft` is read from the main application's preferences and has no CLI override.
+- AC-3: When absent, both keys fall back to empty strings.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:78-82`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0542
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Read Preferences Read-Only
+
+**As a** runtime component
+**I want** the probe to access the main application's preferences read-only
+**So that** it never mutates the user's stored settings
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The probe never writes to the main application's UserDefaults domain.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/cli.md:84-84`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0543
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Persist the selected cleanup model
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** my selected cleanup model to be stored in preferences
+**So that** my choice is restored across launches
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The selected cleanup model is persisted under the `selectedCleanupModelKind` preference key.
+- AC-2: When no stored value exists, the default selection is the 0.8B model.
+- AC-3: After the cleanup model selection changes, the stored preference is updated.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:82-160`
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0544
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Display the model inventory card and model rows
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a card that summarizes model status rows in the required layout
+**So that** I can scan model availability at a glance
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The model inventory card displays a vertical list of model rows inside a rounded rectangle.
+- AC-2: The card uses the system control background color.
+- AC-3: Each row displays a status indicator, the model name, a `Selected` badge when applicable, a status text string, and the model's size description.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:379-426`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0545
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Render model status text and indicators
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** status text and icons that match each model state
+**So that** I can understand model state from the inventory display
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Loaded rows show the status text `Loaded` and a green checkmark circle icon.
+- AC-2: Loading rows show `Loading` and a mini progress spinner.
+- AC-3: Not loaded rows show `Not loaded` and a hollow circle in quaternary color.
+- AC-4: Downloading rows with known progress show `Downloading XX%` using an integer percentage and a pie progress indicator.
+- AC-5: Downloading rows with unknown progress show `Preparing` and a pie progress indicator.
+- AC-6: All status indicators are 14 x 14 points.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:379-426`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0546
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Render the pie progress indicator correctly
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the download progress indicator to visualize determinate and indeterminate progress correctly
+**So that** I can read download progress visually
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: For known progress, the pie renders a filled arc proportional to progress, clamped to a minimum of 0.05 and a maximum of 1.0.
+- AC-2: The determinate arc starts at the 12 o'clock position and sweeps clockwise.
+- AC-3: For indeterminate progress, the pie renders a 28% arc that rotates continuously with a 1-second linear animation loop.
+- AC-4: A background circle stroke at 25% secondary-color opacity is always visible.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:379-426`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0547
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Show active download summary text and row status logic
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** download summaries and row statuses to follow the specified rules
+**So that** I can identify what is actively loading or downloading
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When any model is downloading with determinate progress, the summary line reads `Downloading {name} ({pct}%)...`.
+- AC-2: When any model is downloading with indeterminate progress, the summary line reads `Preparing {name}...`.
+- AC-3: When no model is downloading, the summary line is absent.
+- AC-4: A speech model row shows downloading with progress when the model system is loading, the model is active, and it is not cached.
+- AC-5: A speech model row shows loading when the model system is loading and the model is cached.
+- AC-6: A speech model row shows loaded when cached and not loaded otherwise.
+- AC-7: A cleanup model row shows downloading with progress when that specific variant is being downloaded.
+- AC-8: A cleanup model row shows loading when the model system is loading and the variant matches the selected one.
+- AC-9: A cleanup model row shows loaded when cached and not loaded otherwise.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:379-426`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0548
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Show the core model cards
+
+**As a** user opening Settings > Models
+**I want** see the Speech model, Cleanup model, and Runtime models cards
+**So that** I can manage the models from one place
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Opening Settings > Models shows exactly three cards labeled Speech model, Cleanup model, and Runtime models.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0549
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-016 — Model Inventory & Preferences
+**Title:** Show runtime model inventory status
+
+**As a** user viewing the Models section
+**I want** the Runtime models card to show the cached and loaded state of all speech and cleanup models
+**So that** I can tell which models are available and active
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The Runtime models card displays inventory information for all speech and cleanup models.
+- AC-2: The inventory shows whether each model is cached and whether it is loaded.

+# EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+
+**Summary:** Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Stories:** STORY-0556, STORY-0557, STORY-0558, STORY-0559, STORY-0560, STORY-0561, STORY-0562, STORY-0563, STORY-0564, STORY-0565, STORY-0566, STORY-0567, STORY-0568, STORY-0569, STORY-0570, STORY-0571, STORY-0572, STORY-0573, STORY-0574, STORY-0575, STORY-0576, STORY-0577, STORY-0578, STORY-0579, STORY-0580, STORY-0581, STORY-0582, STORY-0583, STORY-0584, STORY-0585, STORY-0586, STORY-0587, STORY-0588, STORY-0589, STORY-0590, STORY-0591, STORY-0592, STORY-0593, STORY-0594
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md`, `specs/specs/domains/input.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md`
+**Status:** 0/39 done
+
+## STORY-0556
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Bind Default Hotkeys
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the documented default shortcut bindings to exist
+**So that** push-to-talk and toggle-to-talk work without custom setup
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `chordBinding.pushToTalk` defaults to Right Command (54) plus Right Option (61).
+- AC-2: `chordBinding.toggleToTalk` defaults to Right Command (54) plus Right Option (61) plus Space (49).
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md:49-54`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0557
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Encode Chord Bindings
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** keyboard shortcuts to use the documented chord encoding
+**So that** bindings can be serialized and decoded consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Binding keys are constructed as `chordBinding.` plus the action identifier.
+- AC-2: A chord binding serializes as a JSON object containing a set of physical key codes (`UInt16`).
+- AC-3: An empty key set raises a decoding error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md:56-58`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0558
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Expose the canonical key catalog
+
+**As a** keyboard interface
+**I want** to map each listed macOS virtual key code to its display name and category
+**So that** shortcut displays can render the canonical key labels
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Key code 36 displays as `Return` and is non-modifier.
+- AC-2: Key code 48 displays as `Tab` and is non-modifier.
+- AC-3: Key code 49 displays as `Space` and is non-modifier.
+- AC-4: Key code 51 displays as `Delete` and is non-modifier.
+- AC-5: Key code 53 displays as `Escape` and is non-modifier.
+- AC-6: Key codes 54, 55, 56, 57, 58, 59, 60, 61, 62, and 63 map to the listed modifier names and are categorized as modifiers.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:1-24`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0559
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Format unknown key codes consistently
+
+**As a** keyboard interface
+**I want** to display unrecognized key codes as a generic label
+**So that** unknown keys remain readable in the UI
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Any unrecognized key code displays as `Key Code N`, where `N` is the numeric code.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:1-24`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0560
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Recognize the modifier key code range
+
+**As a** hotkey monitor
+**I want** to treat key codes 54 through 63 as modifiers
+**So that** the monitor recognizes exactly the ten modifier key codes imposed by macOS
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The hotkey monitor recognizes exactly key codes 54 through 63 as modifiers.
+- AC-2: No other key codes are treated as modifiers by this contract.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0561
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Detect pressed modifiers from the combined bitmask
+
+**As a** hotkey monitor
+**I want** to interpret modifier key presses from the event flag field
+**So that** modifier state matches the macOS HID bitmask contract
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A modifier is considered pressed when its combined `NX_DEVICE*KEYMASK` bitmask is present in the event's flag field.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0562
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Require non-empty chords
+
+**As a** shortcut bindings
+**I want** to reject empty chord key sets
+**So that** every chord always represents at least one key
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Constructing a chord with an empty key set returns `nil`.
+- AC-2: Deserializing an empty key set raises a decoding error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:36-43`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0563
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Render chords in canonical display order
+
+**As a** shortcut bindings
+**I want** to join chord keys with ` + ` in the canonical sort order
+**So that** users see the same shortcut order everywhere
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Chords are displayed as key names joined by ` + ` separators.
+- AC-2: Command keys are shown first, then Option, Control, Shift, Fn, and then all other keys.
+- AC-3: Ties within a category are broken alphabetically by display name and then by key code.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:45-57`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0564
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Use compact recorder symbols for modifiers
+
+**As a** shortcut recorder
+**I want** to render modifier keys with compact symbols and left/right suffixes
+**So that** the recorder UI can distinguish left and right variants at a glance
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Modifier keys use compact symbols in the shortcut recorder UI.
+- AC-2: Left and right variants use suffixes such as the right superscript form for Right Command.
+- AC-3: Non-modifier keys fall through to their standard display name.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:59-64`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0565
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Provide the default shortcut bindings
+
+**As a** shortcut bindings
+**I want** to use the documented default chords when no preference is stored or decoding fails
+**So that** the app always has usable push-to-talk and toggle-to-talk shortcuts
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Push-to-talk defaults to Right Command + Right Option with key codes {54, 61}.
+- AC-2: Toggle-to-talk defaults to Right Command + Right Option + Space with key codes {54, 61, 49}.
+- AC-3: These defaults are used when no user preference is stored or when stored data cannot be decoded.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:66-75`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0566
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Reject duplicate chord bindings
+
+**As a** shortcut bindings
+**I want** to check all other actions before writing a new binding
+**So that** the same chord cannot be assigned to more than one action
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Before writing a new binding, the store checks all other actions for a binding with the same chord.
+- AC-2: If another action already uses the chord, the write or save is rejected with a duplicate binding error.
+- AC-3: Two actions cannot be persisted with the same chord.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/keyboard-interface.md:77-89`
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:174-209`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0567
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Persist chord bindings in user defaults
+
+**As a** preferences system
+**I want** bindings to be stored in macOS user defaults under action-specific keys
+**So that** shortcut choices survive across launches
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each action's binding is stored in macOS user defaults.
+- AC-2: The storage key is formed by the prefix `chordBinding.` followed by the action name.
+- AC-3: Bindings are serialized as JSON.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:174-209`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0568
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Remove and ignore missing bindings
+
+**As a** preferences system
+**I want** clearing and reading bindings to behave safely when no data exists
+**So that** removed or corrupted shortcuts do not break configuration loading
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Clearing a binding removes its user defaults entry entirely.
+- AC-2: Reading a binding that does not exist returns nothing.
+- AC-3: Reading a binding with corrupted data returns nothing.
+- AC-4: Decode failures are silently ignored.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:174-209`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0569
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Protect against empty chords
+
+**As a** preferences system
+**I want** empty chords to be rejected
+**So that** corrupted or invalid bindings cannot be created or loaded
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Attempting to create a chord with no keys fails.
+- AC-2: Deserializing a chord with an empty key set raises a decoding error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:174-209`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0570
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Ship default shortcut bindings
+
+**As a** new user
+**I want** the application to provide default push and toggle bindings
+**So that** recording works before I customize anything
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The default push-to-talk binding is Right Command + Right Option with key codes 54 and 61.
+- AC-2: The default toggle-to-talk binding is Right Command + Right Option + Space with key codes 54, 61, and 49.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:174-209`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0571
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Roll back failed shortcut changes and rebuild monitoring
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** shortcut updates to revert cleanly when a duplicate is rejected and to refresh monitoring after success
+**So that** the app stays consistent with the saved bindings
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a user changes a shortcut, the system attempts to persist the new binding.
+- AC-2: If the save is rejected due to a duplicate conflict, the change rolls back to the previous values and an error is displayed.
+- AC-3: After a successful binding update, the monitoring system receives the new binding map.
+- AC-4: After a successful binding update, the monitoring system rebuilds its tracked key set and chord engine.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:174-209`
+
+**Status:** pending
