+## STORY-0572
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Represent physical keys by hardware code
+
+**As a** key catalog consumer
+**I want** physical keys to be identified by macOS hardware key codes
+**So that** keys can be compared, hashed, and serialized consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each physical key is identified by a 16-bit unsigned integer hardware key code.
+- AC-2: Keys support equality comparison.
+- AC-3: Keys support hashing.
+- AC-4: Keys support JSON serialization.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:211-247`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0573
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Render recognized display names
+
+**As a** UI renderer
+**I want** known key codes to map to stable display names
+**So that** shortcuts are shown in human-readable form
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Recognized key codes render to the specified display names.
+- AC-2: An unrecognized key code renders as `Key Code N`, where N is the numeric value.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:211-247`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0574
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Sort chord display keys canonically
+
+**As a** UI renderer
+**I want** keys in a chord display to be sorted in canonical order
+**So that** shortcut strings appear consistently across the app
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Command keys sort before Option, Control, Shift, Fn, and all other keys.
+- AC-2: Ties within a category are broken alphabetically by display name.
+- AC-3: If display names are equal, ties are broken by key code.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:211-247`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0575
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Render compact recorder symbols
+
+**As a** shortcut recorder UI
+**I want** modifier keys to use compact symbols and non-modifiers to use standard names
+**So that** the recorder displays shortcuts in a compact form
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: In the shortcut recorder interface, modifier keys use compact symbols with left/right suffixes.
+- AC-2: Non-modifier keys use their standard display name.

+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:211-247`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0576
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Track capture high-water marks
+
+**As a** shortcut capture controller
+**I want** to maintain both the currently pressed set and the captured set
+**So that** the final chord includes every key seen during the session
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: During a shortcut capture session, the system maintains a currently pressed set.
+- AC-2: During a shortcut capture session, the system maintains a captured set that records every key pressed at any point in the session.

+## STORY-0577
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Update capture sets on key events
+
+**As a** shortcut capture controller
+**I want** capture state to respond to modifier and key events correctly
+**So that** the resulting chord matches what the user actually pressed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A modifier flag press toggles the key into the pressed set and also adds it to the captured set.
+- AC-2: A key-down event adds the key to both the pressed set and the captured set.
+- AC-3: A key-up event removes the key from the pressed set only.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:249-281`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0578
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Complete capture when all keys are released
+
+**As a** shortcut capture controller
+**I want** capture to finish when the pressed set becomes empty
+**So that** the full chord can be returned once the user lets go
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the pressed set becomes empty, capture completes.
+- AC-2: On completion, a chord is constructed from all captured keys.
+- AC-3: On completion, both sets are cleared.
+- AC-4: On completion, the constructed chord is returned.
+- AC-5: If any key remains held, the capture session continues.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:249-281`
+
+**Status:** pending

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Suspend monitoring during capture
+
+**As a** monitoring system
+**I want** shortcut capture to suspend chord monitoring and reset the engine
+**So that** capture events do not trigger recording actions
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When shortcut capture begins, chord monitoring is suspended.
+- AC-2: While suspended, keyboard events are logged but produce no chord effects.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:249-281`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0580
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Hold matching off after capture
+
+**As a** monitoring system
+**I want** post-capture matching to stay disabled until it is safe to resume
+**So that** the captured shortcut does not immediately retrigger recording
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After monitoring resumes, if keys are still physically held, chord matching remains disabled.
+- AC-2: Matching stays disabled until all physical keys are released or a fresh single-key press occurs.
+- AC-3: A fresh single-key press is either a modifier change or a key-down where the only pressed key is the new one.
+- AC-4: The guard prevents the just-captured chord from immediately triggering a recording action.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:249-281`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0581
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Validate chord contents
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** require every chord to contain at least one key
+**So that** empty shortcut chords cannot be constructed or decoded
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Constructing a chord with an empty key set fails.
+- AC-2: Deserializing an empty key set raises a decoding error.
+- AC-3: A chord with one or more keys can be represented successfully.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:470-478`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0582
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Render chords canonically
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** see chord key names joined in canonical display order

+

+- AC-2: Chord display orders keys as Command, Option, Control, Shift, Fn, then other keys.
+- AC-3: The shortcut recorder interface has access to a compact symbolic display format.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:470-478`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0583
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Persist chord bindings by action key
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** keyboard chord bindings to be stored under action-derived keys with defaults
+**So that** shortcut configuration survives relaunches and stays tied to each action
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The storage key for a chord binding is built by prefixing `chordBinding.` to the action's raw string value.
+- AC-2: `chordBinding.pushToTalk` defaults to Right Command (54) plus Right Option (61).
+- AC-3: `chordBinding.toggleToTalk` defaults to Right Command (54) plus Right Option (61) plus Space (49).

+## STORY-0584
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Handle invalid or conflicting chord bindings
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** invalid chord data to fall back cleanly and duplicate assignments to be rejected
+**So that** shortcut settings remain consistent and unambiguous
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If stored chord data cannot be decoded as a valid chord, the app reports the binding as not configured.
+- AC-2: If stored chord data cannot be decoded as a valid chord, the app falls back to the hardcoded default chord for that action.
+- AC-3: Before saving a new chord binding, the app validates that the chord is not already assigned to a different action.
+- AC-4: If a chord conflicts with a different action, the write is rejected with a duplicate-binding error.

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts

+**So that** I can tell when it is listening for a chord
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When not recording, the widget shows the current chord's display string using a bordered style with an orange tint.
+- AC-2: When recording starts, the widget switches to bordered prominent filled-orange styling.
+- AC-3: While recording and no keys have been captured yet, the label reads `Press Shortcut`.
+- AC-4: While recording, instructional text below the control reads `Press the full chord, then release. Press Escape to cancel.`

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts

+

+- AC-1: While the widget is recording and one or more keys have already been captured, the button label shows a live preview of the captured keys.

+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:356-357`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0587
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Capture shortcut recording events locally and notify the parent
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the shortcut recorder to intercept key events during capture

+

+- AC-1: Starting a recording installs a local event monitor for modifier-flags-changed, key-down, and key-up events.
+- AC-2: All captured events are consumed and not passed to other responders.
+- AC-3: When recording starts, the widget notifies its parent so the parent can suspend the hotkey monitor during capture.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:350-377`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0588
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Forward non-cancel key-down events during shortcut recording
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** ordinary key-down events to continue through the capture state machine
+**So that** the recorder can build chords from typed keys
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: During shortcut recording, key-down events other than the initial Escape press with no captured keys are passed to the capture state machine.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:365-366`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0589
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Commit and cancel shortcut recordings correctly
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** shortcut recording to commit or cancel based on captured input and lifecycle events
+**So that** the resulting chord is only saved when recording completes successfully
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Modifier-flag changes are passed to the capture state machine using the physical key, and a completed chord ends the recording with a commit.
+- AC-2: Pressing Escape with no captured keys cancels the recording.
+- AC-3: Key-up events are passed to the capture state machine, and a completed chord ends the recording with a commit.
+- AC-4: Committing removes the event monitor, resets capture state, notifies the parent, and invokes the on-change callback with the captured chord.
+- AC-5: Cancelling removes the event monitor and resets capture state without invoking the on-change callback.
+- AC-6: If the view disappears while recording, the recording is cancelled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:350-377`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0590
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Suspend hotkey handling during shortcut capture
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** hotkey processing to pause while I record a shortcut
+**So that** existing chord handling does not interfere with capture
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When shortcut capture starts, the hotkey monitor is suspended.
+- AC-2: While suspended, chord-matching events are ignored.
+- AC-3: The chord engine is reset when capture starts.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md:1-242`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0591
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Record the full shortcut chord
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the recorder to capture every key I press until I release them
+**So that** the saved shortcut reflects the complete chord I entered
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The shortcut capture session records all keys pressed during the capture.
+- AC-2: The capture completes only after all keys are released.
+- AC-3: The constructed chord includes all keys that were pressed during the capture.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md:1-242`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0592
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Resume hotkey handling after capture
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** hotkey handling to resume safely after I finish recording
+**So that** the new chord does not fire prematurely while keys are still held down
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When capture ends, the hotkey monitor is unsuspended.
+- AC-2: If any keys are still physically pressed when the monitor resumes, the new chord does not trigger until all currently pressed keys have been released.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md:1-242`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0593
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Persist a new shortcut binding
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a successfully recorded shortcut to be saved and activated
+**So that** the new binding survives and the hotkey system uses it
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After a new shortcut is recorded, the binding is persisted through the chord binding store.
+- AC-2: The binding is serialized as JSON-encoded data and written to user defaults when no conflict exists.
+- AC-3: The hotkey monitor receives the new binding map and rebuilds its matching state from the updated bindings.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md:1-242`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0594
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-017 — Keyboard Shortcuts
+**Title:** Reject duplicate shortcut bindings
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** duplicate shortcut assignments to be rejected
+**So that** different actions cannot share the same chord
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The chord binding store checks all other actions for an existing binding with the same chord before saving.
+- AC-2: If a duplicate binding exists, the update is rejected with a duplicate binding error.
+- AC-3: On duplicate conflict, the shortcut reverts to its previous value and an error message is displayed in red below the shortcut recorder.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md:1-242`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+
+**Summary:** Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Stories:** STORY-0595, STORY-0596, STORY-0597, STORY-0598, STORY-0599, STORY-0600, STORY-0601, STORY-0602, STORY-0603, STORY-0604, STORY-0605, STORY-0606, STORY-0607, STORY-0608, STORY-0609
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`
+**Status:** 0/15 done
+
+## STORY-0595
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Recover Corrupted Debug Log
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** an unreadable debug log to recover as empty
+**So that** the app can continue without surfacing an error
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A corrupted or unreadable debug log loads as an empty entry list.
+- AC-2: No error is surfaced for that failure.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md:189-191`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0596
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Recover Corrupted Lab Data
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** transcription lab JSON decode failures to trigger destructive recovery
+**So that** corrupted lab data does not remain partially loaded
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If either the index file or the timings file fails to decode, the index file is deleted.
+- AC-2: If either JSON file fails to decode, the timings file is deleted.
+- AC-3: If either JSON file fails to decode, the audio directory and all of its contents are deleted.
+- AC-4: After the destructive cleanup, the load operation returns an empty result.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/configuration.md:193-199`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0597
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Maintain status lifecycle
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the application status to follow the documented lifecycle
+**So that** the UI reflects the real pipeline state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The initial status is Loading.
+- AC-2: The status transitions from Loading to Ready only after the speech model has loaded successfully and the hotkey monitor has started.
+- AC-3: The status transitions from Ready to Recording when recording successfully starts.
+- AC-4: The status transitions from Recording to Transcribing when recording stops and transcription begins.
+- AC-5: If refinement is not available, the status remains Transcribing until delivery completes and then transitions directly to Ready.
+- AC-6: The status returns to Ready after the pipeline completes, even when no transcript was produced.
+- AC-7: The status transitions to Error if recording fails to start or required permissions are missing.
+- AC-8: When the user triggers recording while the status is Loading, the overlay shows `Loading models...` for 1.5 seconds and the recording attempt is silently dropped.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:58-141`
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:162-175`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0598
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Show status-specific menu icons
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the menu bar icon to reflect the current application status
+**So that** status is visible at a glance
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Recording uses the custom red icon.
+- AC-2: Loading uses the orange ellipsis-circle icon.
+- AC-3: Error uses the yellow warning-triangle icon.
+- AC-4: Ready, Transcribing, and Cleaning Up use the custom icon that adapts to the system theme.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:58-141`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0599
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Document status labels and model-load recovery
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the status labels and model-load recovery behavior to match the contract
+**So that** the UI reflects loading and recovery states correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Loading displays `Loading model...`, Ready displays `Ready`, and Error displays `Error`.
+- AC-2: If the speech model fails to load, the status transitions to Error.
+- AC-3: A later successful retry or model load transitions the status from Error back to Ready.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:58-69`
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:71-118`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0600
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Show menu bar recovery actions
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the menu bar dropdown to show error-specific recovery actions
+**So that** I can recover from permission failures quickly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the application has an error message, the menu bar dropdown displays the error text in red.
+- AC-2: If the error text contains `Input Monitoring`, the dropdown shows an `Open Input Monitoring Settings` button that opens the Input Monitoring privacy pane and a `Retry` button that re-attempts starting the hotkey monitor.
+- AC-3: If the error text contains `Accessibility`, the dropdown shows an `Open Accessibility Settings` button that opens the Accessibility privacy pane and a `Retry` button that re-attempts starting the hotkey monitor.
+- AC-4: If the error text contains `Microphone`, the dropdown shows an `Open Microphone Settings` button that opens the Microphone privacy pane.
+- AC-5: No Retry button is shown for microphone errors.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:163-171`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0601
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Run as a menu bar app
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** Ghost Pepper to live in the menu bar without a Dock icon during normal operation
+**So that** it stays unobtrusive
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: During normal operation, the app has no Dock icon.
+- AC-2: The sole persistent interface element is a system tray or menu bar icon with a dropdown panel.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:7-52`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0602
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Render status icons by application state
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the menu bar icon to reflect application status visually
+**So that** I can tell what the app is doing at a glance
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: While recording, the icon displays a custom red-tinted image rendered in its original colors, not as a system template.
+- AC-2: While loading, the icon displays an ellipsis-in-circle system symbol colored orange.
+- AC-3: In an error state, the icon displays a warning triangle system symbol colored yellow.
+- AC-4: In ready, transcribing, and cleaning up states, the icon displays a custom image rendered as a system template so it adapts to light and dark appearance.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:7-52`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0603
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Show the onboarding-only menu
+
+**As a** new user
+**I want** a minimal dropdown before onboarding is complete
+**So that** I only see setup and exit actions
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Before onboarding is complete, the dropdown contains only `Show Setup Window` and `Quit`.
+- AC-2: Selecting `Show Setup Window` brings the onboarding window to the front.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:7-52`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0604
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Open settings and debug log views from the dropdown
+
+**As a** onboarded user
+**I want** dropdown items that open the main settings and debug windows
+**So that** I can reach configuration and diagnostics quickly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After onboarding, `Settings...` opens the settings window.
+- AC-2: After onboarding, `Debug Log...` opens the debug log viewer.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:7-52`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0605
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Show the version label and activity feedback
+
+**As a** onboarded user
+**I want** the dropdown to surface version, status, progress, and errors
+**So that** I can see the app's current state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The version label reads `Ghost Pepper v{version}` using the application bundle version.
+- AC-2: If the bundle version key is missing, the version label defaults to `1.0`.
+- AC-3: The status line reads `Loading...`, `Recording...`, `Transcribing...`, or `Cleaning up...` for those respective states.
+- AC-4: No status line is shown when the app is ready or in an error state.
+- AC-5: When a cleanup model download is in progress, a linear progress bar shows the download progress.
+- AC-6: When an error message is present, the error text is shown in red.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:7-52`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0606
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Preserve the post-onboarding dropdown order
+
+**As a** onboarded user
+**I want** the post-onboarding dropdown items to appear in the documented order
+**So that** the menu is presented consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After onboarding, the dropdown items appear in this order: `Settings...`, `Debug Log...`, `Ghost Pepper v{version}`, the status line, the cleanup download progress bar, and the error text.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:24-38`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0607
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Render contextual recovery buttons
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** error-specific recovery actions in the dropdown
+**So that** I can jump to the relevant system settings or retry the failed action
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the error text contains `Input Monitoring`, the dropdown shows `Open Input Monitoring Settings` and `Retry`.
+- AC-2: When the error text contains `Accessibility`, the dropdown shows `Open Accessibility Settings` and `Retry`.
+- AC-3: When the error text contains `Microphone`, the dropdown shows `Open Microphone Settings`.
+- AC-4: Matching is case-sensitive.
+- AC-5: Multiple recovery button groups may appear at the same time if the error text matches more than one keyword.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:40-46`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0608
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Invoke the documented recovery button actions
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the recovery buttons to open their documented targets and retry the hotkey monitor
+**So that** the buttons actually perform the recovery actions they advertise
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `Open Input Monitoring Settings` opens the system Input Monitoring privacy pane.
+- AC-2: `Retry` for `Input Monitoring` re-attempts the hotkey monitor.
+- AC-3: `Open Accessibility Settings` opens the system Accessibility privacy pane.
+- AC-4: `Open Microphone Settings` opens the system Microphone privacy pane.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:44-46`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0609
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-018 — Menu Bar & Dropdown UI
+**Title:** Separate update and quit footer actions
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the footer actions to be visually separated and the quit action to use the standard shortcut
+**So that** the menu is easy to scan and quit behaves as expected
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A divider separates the error area from `Check for Updates`.
+- AC-2: A second divider separates `Check for Updates` from `Quit`.
+- AC-3: Selecting `Quit` terminates the application.
+- AC-4: `Quit` uses the keyboard shortcut Cmd+Q.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:7-52`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+
+**Summary:** Build & Release Tooling
+**Stories:** STORY-0610, STORY-0611, STORY-0612, STORY-0613, STORY-0614, STORY-0615, STORY-0616, STORY-0617, STORY-0618, STORY-0619, STORY-0620, STORY-0621, STORY-0622, STORY-0623, STORY-0624, STORY-0625, STORY-0626, STORY-0627
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`
+**Status:** 0/18 done
+
+## STORY-0610
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Override cleanup probe derived data path
+
+**As a** build script
+**I want** to use CLEANUP_MODEL_PROBE_DERIVED_DATA when building the cleanup model probe CLI
+**So that** the derived data location can be overridden from the shell environment
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When CLEANUP_MODEL_PROBE_DERIVED_DATA is unset, the build uses `build/cleanup-probe-cli` relative to the project root.
+- AC-2: When CLEANUP_MODEL_PROBE_DERIVED_DATA is set, its value overrides the default derived data path for the cleanup model probe CLI build.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:1-14`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0611
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Detect test environment from XCTest
+
+**As a** permission checker
+**I want** to detect when the app is running under XCTestConfigurationFilePath
+**So that** safe defaults are returned during unit tests instead of calling real system APIs
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the `XCTestConfigurationFilePath` environment variable is present, the permission checker switches to a test client.
+- AC-2: The test client returns safe defaults and does not call real system APIs.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/environment.md:15-20`

+**Status:** pending
+

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling

+**So that** the app uses the required external package contract

+- AC-1: FluidAudio is consumed from `https://github.com/FluidInference/FluidAudio.git`.
+- AC-2: The FluidAudio dependency requires a minimum version of `0.12.4`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:3-5`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0613
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Require WhisperKit source and minimum version
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** WhisperKit to come from the required GitHub source at the minimum supported version
+**So that** the integration uses the mandated upstream package
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: WhisperKit is consumed from `https://github.com/argmaxinc/WhisperKit.git`.

+**So that** the checked-in configuration remains the source of truth

+**Title:** Use the declared project metadata

+

+**Title:** Configure the main application target
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the main app target to match the declared bundle identifier, package dependencies, and app icon
+**So that** the primary product is built with the expected integrations
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The main application bundle identifier is `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper`.
+- AC-2: The main application target depends on WhisperKit, FluidAudio, the LLM product from LLM.swift, and Sparkle.

+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Host unit tests in the app process
+

+**I want** the unit test bundle to run inside the built application process
+**So that** tests exercise the actual app binary

+- AC-1: The unit test bundle is a hosted macOS test bundle that depends on the main application target.

+## STORY-0618
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Configure the cleanup-model probe target
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the cleanup model probe target to match its declared bundle identifier, dependency, and runtime search paths
+**So that** the command-line probe builds and runs with the expected linkage
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The cleanup-model probe bundle identifier is `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper.cleanup-model-probe`.
+- AC-2: The probe target depends only on the LLM product from LLM.swift.
+- AC-3: The runtime library search paths include inherited paths and `@executable_path/Frameworks`.
+

+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0619
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Pin the declared package dependencies
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the package dependencies to use the declared repositories and version constraints
+**So that** build inputs match the documented supply chain
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: WhisperKit comes from `https://github.com/argmaxinc/WhisperKit.git` with a `From 0.16.0` constraint.
+- AC-2: FluidAudio comes from `https://github.com/FluidInference/FluidAudio.git` with a `From 0.12.4` constraint.

+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`

+## STORY-0620
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Ship the DMG build script
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** a shell script that automates the release build and DMG packaging workflow
+**So that** I can produce release artifacts through the documented process
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The DMG build script automates the release build and DMG packaging workflow described in Section 15.4.
+- AC-2: The DMG build script runs with Bash strict error handling enabled via `set -euo pipefail`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:783-793`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0621
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-019 — Build & Release Tooling
+**Title:** Build and run the cleanup probe
+

+**So that** I can probe the cleanup model from the command line without manual setup
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+- AC-2: If the binary is missing, the script builds it with code signing disabled and macro validation skipped.
+- AC-3: The script executes the binary after ensuring it exists and forwards all command-line arguments to it.
+- AC-4: The script honors the `CLEANUP_MODEL_PROBE_DERIVED_DATA` environment variable to override the default derived data location.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:783-793`

+# EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+
+**Summary:** Speech Model Management
+**Stories:** STORY-0628, STORY-0629, STORY-0630, STORY-0631, STORY-0632, STORY-0633, STORY-0634, STORY-0635, STORY-0636, STORY-0637, STORY-0638, STORY-0639, STORY-0640, STORY-0641, STORY-0642, STORY-0643, STORY-0644, STORY-0645, STORY-0646, STORY-0647, STORY-0648, STORY-0649, STORY-0650, STORY-0651, STORY-0652, STORY-0653, STORY-0654, STORY-0655, STORY-0656, STORY-0657, STORY-0658, STORY-0659, STORY-0660, STORY-0661, STORY-0662, STORY-0663
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/speech.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md`
+**Status:** 0/36 done
+
+## STORY-0628
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Download and load the Parakeet model
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to use `AsrModels.downloadAndLoad(version:)` for the Parakeet model
+**So that** speech recognition can initialize from the FluidAudio model package
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The speech subsystem downloads and loads the Parakeet model through `AsrModels.downloadAndLoad(version:)`.
+- AC-2: Progress is reported during the download.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:1-16`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0629
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Require a FluidAudio variant identifier in model descriptors
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to validate that each model descriptor carries a FluidAudio variant identifier
+**So that** missing configuration is rejected instead of silently continuing
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the model descriptor includes a FluidAudio variant identifier, model loading can proceed.
+- AC-2: If the variant identifier is absent, the system fails with a configuration error.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:1-16`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0630
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Initialize the ASR manager from loaded models
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to create and initialize `AsrManager` with the loaded model set
+**So that** speech inference can run against the configured models
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `AsrManager(config:)` creates the manager using the default configuration.
+- AC-2: `asrManager.init(models:)` initializes the manager with an `AsrModels` instance.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:18-24`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0631
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Transcribe microphone audio buffers
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to call `asrManager.transcribe(audioBuffer:source:)` with microphone audio
+**So that** speech-to-text inference can run on live microphone input
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Transcription is invoked with a float audio buffer.
+- AC-2: The source indicator passed to transcription is `.microphone`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:18-24`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0632
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Normalize FluidAudio transcription output
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to trim whitespace from FluidAudio transcription results and reject empty text
+**So that** blank results are treated as no transcription
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Transcription text from FluidAudio is trimmed of whitespace.
+- AC-2: If the trimmed result is empty, the system returns `nil`.
+- AC-3: Artifact removal is not applied to FluidAudio transcription results.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:18-24`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0633
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Consume the FluidAudio parakeet catalog entry
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to use `fluid_parakeet-v3` as the FluidAudio model catalog entry
+**So that** speaker filtering is available from the only supported speaker-filtering model
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The consumed model ID is `fluid_parakeet-v3`.
+- AC-2: The model uses a FluidAudio variant identifier.
+- AC-3: The model size is about 1.4 GB.
+- AC-4: The model supports 25 languages.
+- AC-5: This is the only model in the catalog that supports speaker filtering.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:44-52`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0634
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Limit FluidAudio consumption to the single documented model
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to treat `fluid_parakeet-v3` as the only FluidAudio model consumed by the app
+**So that** no other FluidAudio models are introduced into the contract
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app consumes `fluid_parakeet-v3` as the only FluidAudio model.
+- AC-2: No other FluidAudio model entries are consumed by the app.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:44-52`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0635
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Manage model load state
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** follow the defined model state machine
+**So that** loading behavior is predictable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The subsystem starts in the idle state with no model loaded, no error present, and readiness reported as false.
+- AC-2: A load request from idle transitions to loading.
+- AC-3: A load request from error transitions to loading.
+- AC-4: A successful load transitions from loading to ready.
+- AC-5: A retryable load failure stays in loading for the retry attempt.
+- AC-6: A non-retryable failure or failed retry transitions to error.
+- AC-7: Requesting the same model while ready is a no-op.
+- AC-8: Requesting a different model while ready resets to idle before loading the new model.
+- AC-9: A load request while already loading is ignored.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:53-132`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0636
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Resolve model load requests
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** resolve load requests against the configured model and catalog before starting a load
+**So that** unnamed requests use the stored preference and invalid requests fail cleanly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If a model load is requested without an explicit model identifier, the system uses the currently configured model.
+- AC-2: If the requested identifier does not match any catalog entry, the system transitions to the error state with a not found error and does not attempt a download.
+- AC-3: When a load request proceeds into loading, the system clears any prior error before starting the load.
+- AC-4: When a load request enters the loading state, the system logs the attempt.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:53-132`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0637
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Load Whisper models from cache or download
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** ensure Whisper models are present on disk and initialize WhisperKit with the required options
+**So that** Whisper transcription can run from the configured model
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The system ensures a `GhostPepper/whisper-models/` directory exists under application support.
+- AC-2: When the Whisper model is not cached, it is downloaded via WhisperKit.
+- AC-3: Download progress is reported as a fraction from 0.0 to 1.0.
+- AC-4: The progress indicator is cleared once the download completes.
+- AC-5: After download or cache hit, the WhisperKit engine is initialized with verbose output disabled, error-only logging, pre-warming disabled, and loading enabled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:53-132`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0638
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Load FluidAudio models with a variant id
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** download and initialize FluidAudio models using the required variant identifier
+**So that** FluidAudio transcription can run correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A FluidAudio model without a variant identifier fails with a configuration error.
+- AC-2: FluidAudio models are downloaded and loaded via the FluidAudio SDK.
+- AC-3: Download progress is reported during the FluidAudio load.
+- AC-4: After download, the system creates a FluidAudio speech recognition manager with default configuration.
+- AC-5: After download, the manager is initialized with the loaded models.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:53-132`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0639
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Retry timed-out model loads once
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** retry model loads when the failure is a timeout
+**So that** transient network failures can recover automatically
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A URL loading error with a timed-out code is treated as retryable.
+- AC-2: Any error whose human-readable description contains `timed out` case-insensitively is treated as retryable.
+- AC-3: Wrapped underlying errors are inspected recursively for retryability.
+- AC-4: When retryable, all loaded engine instances are cleared before retrying.
+- AC-5: When retryable, the system waits 500 milliseconds before retrying.
+- AC-6: Exactly one retry is attempted.
+- AC-7: If the retry also fails, the system transitions to the error state normally.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:53-132`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0640
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-020 — Speech Model Management
+**Title:** Report cached models
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** check whether model files already exist without downloading and report the cached model set
+**So that** the UI and loader can avoid unnecessary work
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Whisper cache checks use the expected directory under the whisper models root.
+- AC-2: FluidAudio cache checks delegate to the FluidAudio framework's cache directory mechanism.
+- AC-3: The system provides the set of all currently cached model identifiers.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:53-132`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+
+**Summary:** Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Stories:** STORY-0664, STORY-0665, STORY-0666, STORY-0667, STORY-0668, STORY-0669, STORY-0670, STORY-0671, STORY-0672, STORY-0673, STORY-0674, STORY-0675, STORY-0676, STORY-0677, STORY-0678, STORY-0679, STORY-0680, STORY-0681, STORY-0682, STORY-0683, STORY-0684, STORY-0685, STORY-0686, STORY-0687, STORY-0688, STORY-0689, STORY-0690, STORY-0691, STORY-0692, STORY-0693, STORY-0694, STORY-0695, STORY-0696, STORY-0697, STORY-0698, STORY-0699, STORY-0700, STORY-0701, STORY-0702, STORY-0703
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md`, `specs/specs/domains/speech.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md`
+**Status:** 0/40 done
+
+## STORY-0664
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Cache Sortformer models for reuse
+
+**As a** diarization subsystem
+**I want** to load Sortformer models lazily from HuggingFace and reuse them across coordinators
+**So that** subsequent recording coordinators do not reload the model unnecessarily
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `SortformerModels.load(from: .huggingFace)` downloads and loads the Sortformer diarization models.
+- AC-2: The loaded Sortformer models are cached lazily.
+- AC-3: Once loaded, the same models are reused for subsequent coordinators.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0665
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Create one diarizer per recording session coordinator
+
+**As a** recording session coordinator
+**I want** to instantiate `SortformerDiarizer` from loaded models
+**So that** each recording session uses its own diarizer instance
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `SortformerDiarizer(models:)` creates a diarizer instance from loaded models.
+- AC-2: The coordinator creates one diarizer instance per recording session.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0666
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Feed every converted chunk into the diarizer
+
+**As a** recording session coordinator
+**I want** to call `diarizer.processChunk(audioChunk:)` for each converted audio chunk
+**So that** speaker analysis stays current during recording
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each converted audio chunk is passed to `diarizer.processChunk(audioChunk:)` during recording.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0667
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Finalize diarization when recording stops
+
+**As a** recording session coordinator
+**I want** to call `diarizer.finalize()` when recording ends
+**So that** the speaker timeline can be produced and resources can be released
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `diarizer.finalize()` is called when recording stops.
+- AC-2: Finalization produces the speaker timeline.
+- AC-3: Diarizer resources are released after finalization in the coordinator finish path.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0668
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Flatten diarizer output into sorted spans
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to convert grouped diarizer segments into flat diarization spans
+**So that** the result can be consumed as a normalized timeline
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Diarizer output grouped by speaker index is converted into a flat diarization span array.
+- AC-2: Speaker identifiers are synthesized as `Speaker 0`, `Speaker 1`, and so on.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0669
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Order diarization spans deterministically
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to sort diarization spans by start time with an end-time tie-breaker
+**So that** speaker timelines are stable and predictable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The resulting spans are sorted by start time.
+- AC-2: When start times tie, spans are ordered by end time.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:26-34`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0670
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Fall back when no usable speaker spans exist
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to detect the case where diarization produces no usable spans
+**So that** full-buffer transcription is used instead
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When no usable speaker spans are available, the system falls back to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:69-78`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0671
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Fall back when no speaker reaches the threshold
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to detect when no speaker meets the 0.5 second duration threshold
+**So that** full-buffer transcription is used instead
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When no speaker's total duration reaches the 0.5 s threshold, the system falls back to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:69-78`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0672
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Fall back when kept audio is too short
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to detect when merged kept audio is below the minimum duration
+**So that** full-buffer transcription is used instead
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the merged kept audio is below 0.75 s, the system falls back to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:69-78`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0673
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Fall back when filtered audio extraction fails
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to detect extraction failures from the audio buffer
+**So that** full-buffer transcription is used instead
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When sample extraction from the buffer fails, the system falls back to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:69-78`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0674
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Fall back when filtered transcription is empty
+
+**As a** speaker filtering pipeline
+**I want** to detect an empty transcription result from extracted audio
+**So that** full-buffer transcription is used instead
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When transcription of the extracted audio returns empty, the system falls back to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:69-78`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0675
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Prefer speaker-filtered transcription
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** speaker filtering to run before fallback full-buffer transcription
+**So that** the target speaker's audio is preferred when filtering is available
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If ignore-other-speakers is enabled and the active speech model supports speaker filtering, a speaker filtering session is prepared to receive audio chunks in real time.
+- AC-2: If speaker filtering is active, the target speaker's audio segments are resolved and transcribed before any fallback transcription is attempted.
+- AC-3: If speaker filtering fails or is inactive, the full audio buffer is transcribed.
+- AC-4: The documented WhisperKit models are not treated as speaker-filtering capable.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:13-56`
+- `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md:1-83`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0676
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Create diarization coordinators only when eligible
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** produce a recording session coordinator only when the configured model, speaker-filtering support, and FluidAudio engine are all available
+**So that** speaker filtering is enabled only when it can actually work
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the configured model does not exist in the catalog, no coordinator is produced.
+- AC-2: If the model does not support speaker filtering, no coordinator is produced.
+- AC-3: If the FluidAudio engine is not loaded, no coordinator is produced.
+- AC-4: A coordinator is produced only when all three conditions are met.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0677
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Cache diarization models and degrade gracefully
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** load diarization models lazily and reuse them from memory
+**So that** coordinator creation avoids unnecessary downloads
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Diarization models are downloaded from a remote repository when first needed.
+- AC-2: Once loaded, subsequent coordinator creations reuse cached diarization models without re-downloading.
+- AC-3: If diarization model loading fails, the error is logged.
+- AC-4: If diarization model loading fails, no coordinator is returned.
+- AC-5: If diarization model loading fails, the system degrades to full-buffer transcription.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0678
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Wire diarization into the session pipeline
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** accumulate each audio chunk in the speech session and feed it to the diarizer
+**So that** real-time speaker analysis can be finalized correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each audio chunk is accumulated in the speech session.
+- AC-2: Each audio chunk is simultaneously fed to the diarizer for real-time speaker analysis.
+- AC-3: On finish, the diarizer timeline is finalized.
+- AC-4: On finish, diarizer segments are converted to diarization spans and passed to the speech session finalization pipeline.
+- AC-5: After finalization, diarizer resources are cleaned up.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0679
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Continue after chunk diarization errors
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** catch errors from individual diarizer chunk operations and keep processing
+**So that** one bad chunk does not abort the whole diarization flow
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the diarizer throws while processing an individual audio chunk, the error is caught and logged.
+- AC-2: After a chunk-level diarizer error, subsequent chunks continue to be processed.
+- AC-3: A single chunk failure does not abort overall diarization.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0680
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Convert diarization spans canonically
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** flatten diarizer output into sorted diarization spans with stable speaker labels
+**So that** the downstream session receives normalized spans
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Raw diarizer output is converted into flat diarization spans.
+- AC-2: Speaker identifiers are synthesized as `Speaker 0`, `Speaker 1`, and so on.
+- AC-3: The resulting spans are sorted by start time.
+- AC-4: Ties are broken by end time.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0681
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Finalize diarization through either path
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** support both self-contained and externally supplied finalization paths
+**So that** the coordinator can finish with or without an internal diarizer
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a diarizer is wired in, calling finish with no arguments finalizes the diarizer internally and passes its spans to the speech session.
+- AC-2: When no diarizer is wired in, calling finish with explicit spans passes those spans directly to the speech session.
+- AC-3: If the self-contained finalization handler is absent, finish returns a fallback summary indicating no usable speaker spans.
+- AC-4: The coordinator stores the filtered transcript, if any, after finalization.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0682
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Choose the speaker-filtered transcript at stop time
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** prefer the filtered transcript when diarization finalization succeeds
+**So that** speaker-filtered results are used whenever they are available
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If a recording session coordinator exists, stop time calls its finish method to finalize diarization.
+- AC-2: If finalization succeeds and a non-empty filtered transcript was produced, that filtered transcript becomes the raw transcription.
+- AC-3: If finalization falls back or produces no filtered transcript, the system falls through to full-buffer transcription via the serialized transcriber.
+- AC-4: Regardless of whether speaker filtering was usable, the diarization summary is preserved for archival.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:185-243`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0683
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Finalize speaker-filtered transcription
+
+**As a** caller of the finalization pipeline
+**I want** finalization to return a filtered transcript plus diarization summary when the pipeline succeeds
+**So that** I can distinguish successful filtered output from fallback cases
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The finalization method accepts an array of diarization spans.
+- AC-2: The finalization result contains an optional filtered transcript and a diarization summary.
+- AC-3: The pipeline evaluates its steps in the documented order.
+- AC-4: If any guarded step fails, finalization returns a fallback result with the documented reason.
+- AC-5: If all steps pass, finalization returns the filtered transcript and marks fallback as not used.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:281-314`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0684
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Sort diarization spans by start then end time
+
+**As a** speaker-filtered transcription pipeline
+**I want** input spans to be sorted by start time with end time as the tie-breaker
+**So that** finalization processes spans in a deterministic order
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: During finalization, all input spans are sorted by start time.
+- AC-2: When two spans have the same start time, the span with the earlier end time is ordered first.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:284-286`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0685
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Select the target speaker chronologically
+
+**As a** speaker-filtered transcription pipeline
+**I want** the target speaker to be chosen by first qualifying appearance
+**So that** selection is based on chronology, not raw total speaking time alone
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The pipeline accumulates total speaking duration per speaker across all spans before selecting a target speaker.
+- AC-2: The pipeline iterates spans in chronological order after sorting by start time.
+- AC-3: The target speaker is the first speaker encountered whose total accumulated duration is at least 0.5 seconds.
+- AC-4: If no speaker reaches the threshold, target speaker selection returns nothing.
+- AC-5: The selected target speaker is not determined solely by which speaker has the most total speaking time.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:316-328`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0686
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Mark target-speaker spans as kept
+
+**As a** speaker-filtered transcription pipeline
+**I want** spans matching the selected target speaker to be marked as kept
+**So that** only the target speaker's audio is retained for later merging and extraction
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: During finalization, every span matching the selected target speaker is marked as kept.
+- AC-2: During finalization, spans that do not match the selected target speaker remain unmarked.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:301-304`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0687
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Merge kept spans by gap tolerance
+
+**As a** speaker-filtered transcription pipeline
+**I want** kept spans to be merged with the documented gap tolerance rules
+**So that** retained audio is combined consistently before extraction
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Only spans marked as kept are considered for merging.
+- AC-2: Zero-duration spans are skipped.
+- AC-3: If the gap between a previous merged span and the current span is 0.05 seconds or less, the spans are merged by extending the previous end time to the maximum end time.
+- AC-4: If the gap exceeds 0.05 seconds, a new merged span begins.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:330-339`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0688
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Extract kept audio samples
+
+**As a** speaker-filtered transcription pipeline
+**I want** audio extraction to map merged spans to sample slices deterministically
+**So that** transcription receives the correct kept audio
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Audio extraction reads the accumulated audio buffer under thread-safe synchronization.
+- AC-2: If the audio buffer is empty, extraction returns nothing.
+- AC-3: Start times are converted to sample indices using floor rounding and end times using ceiling rounding at the configured sample rate, which defaults to 16,000 Hz.
+- AC-4: Sample indices are clamped to valid buffer bounds.
+- AC-5: Spans whose end index does not exceed their start index are skipped.
+- AC-6: Extracted sample slices are concatenated into a single output buffer.
+- AC-7: If the output buffer is empty, extraction returns nothing.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:341-348`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0689
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Apply speaker-filter configuration defaults
+
+**As a** pipeline builder
+**I want** speaker-filtered transcription to accept the documented configuration parameters
+**So that** the pipeline runs with the specified thresholds and callback requirement
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The pipeline accepts a sample rate parameter that defaults to 16,000 Hz.
+- AC-2: The pipeline accepts a substantial speaker threshold parameter that defaults to 0.5 seconds.
+- AC-3: The pipeline accepts a minimum kept audio duration parameter that defaults to 0.75 seconds.
+- AC-4: The pipeline accepts a merge gap tolerance parameter that defaults to 0.05 seconds.
+- AC-5: The pipeline requires an async transcription callback that transcribes a float audio buffer into text.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:341-348`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0690
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Populate diarization summary state
+
+**As a** caller inspecting speaker filtering results
+**I want** the diarization summary to capture the complete filtering attempt state
+**So that** I can inspect what happened during finalization
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The summary includes all diarization spans with speaker attribution and kept/not-kept marking.
+- AC-2: The summary includes merged kept spans.
+- AC-3: The summary includes the identified target speaker when one exists.
+- AC-4: The summary includes the target speaker's total speaking duration.
+- AC-5: The summary includes the total kept audio duration.
+- AC-6: The summary indicates whether fallback to full transcription was used.
+- AC-7: The summary includes the fallback reason when fallback was used.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:350-364`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0691
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Define diarization span properties
+
+**As a** consumer of diarization data
+**I want** each diarization span to expose the documented properties
+**So that** span state is testable and consistent
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each diarization span carries a speaker identifier string.
+- AC-2: Each diarization span carries start and end time intervals.
+- AC-3: Each diarization span carries a kept/not-kept flag that defaults to not kept.
+- AC-4: Each diarization span has a computed duration equal to the greater of zero and end minus start.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:366-372`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0692
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Define merged span properties
+
+**As a** consumer of merged diarization data
+**I want** each merged span to expose start, end, and computed duration
+**So that** merged audio ranges remain inspectable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each merged span carries start and end time intervals.
+- AC-2: Each merged span has a computed duration using the same greater-of-zero formula as regular spans.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:374-376`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0693
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-021 — Speaker Filtering & Diarization
+**Title:** Expose the five fallback reasons
+
+**As a** caller handling filtered transcription fallback
+**I want** fallback outcomes to use exactly the documented reasons
+**So that** I can distinguish each failure mode reliably
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The only defined fallback reasons are: No usable speaker spans, No speaker reached threshold, Insufficient kept audio, Filtered audio extraction failed, and Empty filtered transcription.
+- AC-2: No additional fallback reasons are defined.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/speech.md:378-387`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0694
+

+# EPIC-022 — Model Management
+
+**Summary:** Model Management
+**Stories:** STORY-0704, STORY-0705, STORY-0706, STORY-0707, STORY-0708, STORY-0709, STORY-0710, STORY-0711, STORY-0712, STORY-0713, STORY-0714, STORY-0715, STORY-0716, STORY-0717, STORY-0718, STORY-0719, STORY-0720, STORY-0721, STORY-0722, STORY-0723, STORY-0724, STORY-0725, STORY-0726, STORY-0727, STORY-0728, STORY-0729, STORY-0730, STORY-0731, STORY-0732, STORY-0733
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md`, `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md`, `specs/specs/domains/speech.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md`
+**Status:** 0/30 done
+
+## STORY-0704
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Delegate FluidAudio model caching to the framework
+
+**As a** speech subsystem
+**I want** to use FluidAudio's own cache directory mechanism
+**So that** model storage stays outside the application's GhostPepper application support tree
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: FluidAudio model caching is delegated to the framework's own cache directory mechanism.
+- AC-2: The cache path is not under `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/fluidaudio-integration.md:36-43`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0705
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Cache WhisperKit models
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** WhisperKit models to download into the expected cache location only when they are not already cached
+**So that** model loading reuses on-disk files and avoids unnecessary downloads
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When the whisper model root does not exist, the system creates `~/Library/Application Support/GhostPepper/whisper-models/` before starting a download.
+- AC-2: A model is treated as cached only when the directory built from its cache path components exists under the whisper models root.
+- AC-3: When a model is not cached, the system calls `WhisperKit.download(variant:downloadBase:useBackgroundSession:)`.
+- AC-4: Download progress is exposed as a fraction from 0.0 to 1.0 and is cleared after the download completes.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md:1-83`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0706
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Configure WhisperKit engine
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** the WhisperKit engine to initialize with the required configuration
+**So that** transcription runs with the expected runtime behavior
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The engine is created with `WhisperKit(WhisperKitConfig(...))`.
+- AC-2: The configuration uses `verbose: false`, `logLevel: error-only`, `prewarm: false`, and `load: true`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md:1-83`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0707
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Use the supported WhisperKit variants
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** to rely only on the documented WhisperKit model variants
+**So that** model selection and language behavior stay within the contract
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The app consumes only `openai_whisper-tiny.en`, `openai_whisper-small.en`, and `openai_whisper-small` as WhisperKit-backed models.
+- AC-2: `openai_whisper-tiny.en` and `openai_whisper-small.en` are treated as English-only models.
+- AC-3: `openai_whisper-small` is treated as multilingual.
+- AC-4: None of the three WhisperKit models is treated as supporting speaker filtering.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/contracts/whisperkit-integration.md:1-83`
+
+**Status:** pending
+

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management

+**I want** choose from exactly four speech recognition models

+## STORY-0726
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Track model lifecycle states
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** cleanup models to move through the documented lifecycle states
+**So that** load and unload behavior is predictable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The lifecycle states are Idle, Downloading, Loading, Ready, and Error.
+- AC-2: Idle is the initial state and the state after explicit unload.
+- AC-3: A load request in Idle with no file on disk moves the model to Downloading.
+- AC-4: A load request in Idle with the file already on disk moves the model to Loading.
+- AC-5: A load request in Idle when availability override is false moves the model to Error.
+- AC-6: A successful download transitions the model from Downloading to Loading.
+- AC-7: A failed download transitions the model from Downloading to Error.
+- AC-8: A successful initialization transitions the model from Loading to Ready.
+- AC-9: A failed initialization transitions the model from Loading to Error.
+- AC-10: A load request in Ready for the same model is a no-op.
+- AC-11: A load request in Ready for a different model begins a new Downloading or Loading path.
+- AC-12: An unload request in Ready transitions the model to Idle.
+- AC-13: A load request in Error retries via Downloading or Loading.
+- AC-14: A shutdown request transitions the model to Idle and releases all resources.
+- AC-15: While a model is Downloading, its progress value ranges from 0.0 to 1.0.
+- AC-16: When download or initialization fails, the Error state exposes a human-readable message describing the failure.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:82-160`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0727
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Wait for in-flight loads
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** concurrent load requests to wait for the current load to finish
+**So that** only one load sequence runs at a time
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a load is requested while another load is already in progress, the requester waits by polling at 10 ms intervals until the in-progress load resolves.
+- AC-2: If the loaded model matches the request, the waiting requester proceeds without starting a new load.
+- AC-3: If the loaded model does not match the request, a new load begins after the in-progress load resolves.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:82-160`
+
+**Status:** pending
+

+

+- AC-2: After loading, the model thinking mode is set to suppressed.
+- AC-3: At load time, the system prompt is initialized with the default cleanup prompt.
+- AC-4: Before each inference, the system prompt is overwritten with the active assembled prompt.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:82-160`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0729

+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management

+
+**As a** system

+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/text-processing.md:82-160`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0730
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-022 — Model Management
+**Title:** Keep downloaded models cached permanently
+
+**As a** user who has downloaded models
+**I want** the models to remain on disk
+**So that** I do not need to download them again
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Downloaded models remain cached on disk.
+- AC-2: The app does not automatically evict or expire cached models.

+**So that** cleanup inference only proceeds when model loading succeeds
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The application creates the model instance with `LLM(from:maxTokenCount:)` using a local file URL and the configured max token count.
+- AC-2: If `LLM(from:maxTokenCount:)` returns nil, the load is treated as a failure.

+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After loading, the model temperature is set to 0.1.
+- AC-2: The active cleanup prompt is set as the system prompt before each inference.
+- AC-3: The incremental update callback is installed as a no-op.

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+**So that** backend resources are released cleanly

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+**So that** concurrent cleanup requests do not run simultaneously

+**Acceptance criteria:**

+# EPIC-024 — App Updates
+

+**Stories:** STORY-0756, STORY-0757, STORY-0758, STORY-0759, STORY-0760, STORY-0761, STORY-0762, STORY-0763, STORY-0764, STORY-0765, STORY-0766, STORY-0767, STORY-0768, STORY-0769, STORY-0770, STORY-0771, STORY-0772, STORY-0773, STORY-0774, STORY-0775, STORY-0776, STORY-0777, STORY-0778, STORY-0779, STORY-0780, STORY-0781, STORY-0782, STORY-0783

+**So that** the updater subsystem does not start until first use
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: `SPUStandardUpdaterController(startingUpdater:updaterDelegate:userDriverDelegate:)` is wrapped in lazy initialization.
+- AC-2: The Sparkle subsystem does not start until the controller is first accessed.

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0765

+## STORY-0766
+
