+## STORY-0767
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Trigger manual update checks from the menu
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a menu bar action to check for updates manually
+**So that** I can request an update check on demand
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The menu bar dropdown includes a `Check for Updates` button.
+- AC-2: Activating the button triggers a manual update check via the Sparkle updater.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:394-431`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0768
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Publish a valid single-item appcast feed
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** the appcast to conform to the documented RSS and Sparkle format
+**So that** Sparkle can detect and verify the latest release
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The appcast is a valid RSS 2.0 document.
+- AC-2: The appcast declares the Sparkle XML namespace and the Dublin Core namespace.
+- AC-3: The channel title is `Ghost Pepper`.
+- AC-4: The channel link points to the GitHub repository.
+- AC-5: The feed advertises only a single item representing the latest version.
+- AC-6: Each item includes a human-readable title, an internal build number, a short marketing version string, a minimum system version, and an enclosure.
+- AC-7: The enclosure uses MIME type `application/octet-stream`.
+- AC-8: The enclosure includes an EdDSA signature in the `sparkle:edSignature` attribute.
+- AC-9: The enclosure includes the file size in bytes.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:394-431`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0769
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Include DMG metadata in the appcast
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** `appcast.xml` to include the artifact metadata required for Sparkle
+**So that** the feed contains the released DMG details
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Updating `appcast.xml` includes the DMG size in bytes.
+- AC-2: Updating `appcast.xml` includes the EdDSA signature.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:747-747`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0770
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Keep version numbers aligned for Sparkle
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** the marketing version and build number to be set in Info.plist and mirrored in the appcast
+**So that** Sparkle compares updates correctly and does not loop
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Both the marketing version and build number are present in Info.plist and in the appcast for each release.
+- AC-2: The build number is the value used by Sparkle for update comparison.
+- AC-3: A mismatch between the Info.plist values and the appcast values causes an update loop.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0771
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Apply the declared Info.plist values
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** Info.plist to contain the declared bundle metadata and update settings
+**So that** the app is signed, updated, and branded correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The bundle display name is `Ghost Pepper`.
+- AC-2: The bundle identifier is `com.github.matthartman.ghostpepper`.
+- AC-3: The marketing version is `1.9`.
+- AC-4: The build number is `10`.
+- AC-5: `LSUIElement` is `true`.
+- AC-6: `SUFeedURL` is `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/matthartman/ghost-pepper/main/appcast.xml`.
+- AC-7: `SUPublicEDKey` is `aU72TC8lOwqKtnWlwAkT8z7LhIvB+LRnUNRNNw9DmDQ=`.
+- AC-8: `NSMicrophoneUsageDescription` is `Ghost Pepper needs microphone access to record your voice for transcription.`
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:638-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0772
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Follow the manual release workflow
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** releases to follow the documented manual sequence
+**So that** versioning, packaging, publishing, and distribution stay consistent
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The release workflow updates Info.plist, runs the build script, notarizes the DMG, updates appcast.xml, commits and pushes the appcast changes to `main`, and creates a GitHub release with the DMG attached.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:740-753`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0773
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Keep releases out of CI/CD
+
+**As a** release engineer
+**I want** the repository to have no CI/CD configuration
+**So that** the documented release process remains entirely manual
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: No CI/CD configuration exists in the repository.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:740-753`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0774
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Run scheduled and manual update checks
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** the app to check for updates automatically and on demand
+**So that** I can receive new versions without manual version hunting
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The auto-updater checks for updates automatically at a 24-hour interval.
+- AC-2: The automatic check fetches the appcast XML from the configured GitHub URL and parses it as RSS 2.0.
+- AC-3: Clicking "Check for Updates" triggers an immediate update check via the auto-updater.
+- AC-4: The manual check performs the same version comparison as the automatic flow.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0775
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Compare update versions and OS compatibility
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** update availability to be determined from build numbers and system compatibility
+**So that** I only see updates that can actually run on my Mac
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The updater reads the appcast item version element as the internal build number.
+- AC-2: An update is available only when the appcast build number is higher than the running application's build version.
+- AC-3: The updater verifies that the appcast minimum system version is matched or below the current OS version.
+- AC-4: If the current OS version is below the minimum system version, the update is not offered.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0776
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Show the built-in update dialogs
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** the updater to present the standard update UI and the up-to-date dialog
+**So that** I get clear choices and status feedback
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When an update is available, the updater presents its standard built-in update UI rather than custom Ghost Pepper UI.
+- AC-2: The dialog shows the new version title and the short version string.
+- AC-3: The dialog offers install, skip, and remind later options.
+- AC-4: When no update is available, the updater displays a "You're up to date" dialog.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0777
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Download and verify the update DMG
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** the installer to verify the downloaded update package before installation
+**So that** corrupted or tampered updates are rejected
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The updater downloads the DMG from the GitHub release asset URL.
+- AC-2: The updater verifies the DMG's EdDSA signature using the public key embedded in the application's property list.
+- AC-3: If the signature is invalid or the DMG is corrupted, the update is rejected and an error is shown.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0778
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Install and relaunch the updated app
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** the app to replace itself with the downloaded version and relaunch
+**So that** I end up on the updated build without manual reinstall steps
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The updater extracts the new application bundle from the DMG.
+- AC-2: The old application bundle is replaced with the new one.
+- AC-3: The application terminates and relaunches with the updated version.
+- AC-4: The relaunch uses a system open command with the application bundle path to spawn the new instance.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0779
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Preserve update configuration and user data
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** my updater settings and app data to survive the update
+**So that** an installed update does not wipe my local state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After an update, the updater still points to the same appcast URL.
+- AC-2: After an update, the updater still uses the same EdDSA public key.
+- AC-3: User preferences, model caches, correction store data, and lab archives persist across the update.
+- AC-4: The updated application's build version and marketing version reflect the new values.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0780
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Handle update failures gracefully
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** update failures to fail safely
+**So that** a broken network or bad signature does not leave the app unusable
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the appcast cannot be fetched during a scheduled check, the check silently fails and retries at the next 24-hour interval.
+- AC-2: During a manual check, an appcast fetch failure may show an error dialog.
+- AC-3: If the DMG signature does not match the embedded public key, the updater rejects the update and the current version keeps running.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0781
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Build and publish release artifacts
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the release process to produce the signed DMG and updated appcast entry
+**So that** users can receive a verifiable update from GitHub
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The build-DMG script reads the version and build number from the property list.
+- AC-2: The build-DMG script builds a release configuration and creates a compressed DMG.
+- AC-3: The build-DMG script generates an EdDSA signature and prints the DMG path, size in bytes, and next steps.
+- AC-4: The appcast XML is updated with the new version number, build number, DMG size, download URL, and EdDSA signature.
+- AC-5: The appcast update is committed and pushed to main.
+- AC-6: A GitHub release is created with the DMG attached using `gh release create`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0782
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Defer auto-updater initialization until the menu bar is shown
+
+**As a** application user
+**I want** the auto-updater to initialize only after the full menu bar view is first displayed after onboarding
+**So that** update infrastructure does not start before the app is ready for it
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The auto-updater remains lazily uninitialized until onboarding is complete.
+- AC-2: The first display of the full menu bar view after onboarding triggers auto-updater initialization.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0783
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-024 — App Updates
+**Title:** Notarize the release DMG with Apple
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** the release DMG to be notarized with Apple before distribution
+**So that** the published update is accepted by macOS
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The developer notarizes the DMG with Apple as part of the release workflow.
+- AC-2: Notarization happens before the appcast is updated and before the GitHub release is created.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/app-update.md:1-149`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+
+**Summary:** Performance Tracing
+**Stories:** STORY-0784, STORY-0785, STORY-0786, STORY-0787, STORY-0788, STORY-0789, STORY-0790, STORY-0791, STORY-0792, STORY-0793, STORY-0794, STORY-0795, STORY-0796, STORY-0797, STORY-0798
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/debug-investigation.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md`
+**Status:** 0/15 done
+
+## STORY-0784
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Record the performance trace
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** the performance trace to capture the documented timestamps and completion data
+**So that** session timing can be inspected after the fact
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A trace is created with a unique session identifier and the hotkey detection timestamp at the start of the session.
+- AC-2: The trace records the hotkey release timestamp, transcription start timestamp, transcription end timestamp, and paste-end timestamp at the documented points.
+- AC-3: If context capture runs, its duration is recorded on the trace.
+- AC-4: When the session completes, the full trace summary is written to the debug log and the trace is cleared.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:13-56`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0785
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Create a trace on first hotkey press
+
+**As a** user starting a recording session
+**I want** a new performance trace to be created when I first press the push-to-talk or toggle-to-talk hotkey
+**So that** the session has trace metadata from the start
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a push-to-talk or toggle-to-talk hotkey is first pressed, a new performance trace is created.
+- AC-2: The created trace contains a unique session identifier.
+- AC-3: The created trace contains the hotkey detection timestamp.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:199-260`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0786
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Create a fallback trace before pipeline acquisition
+
+**As a** system starting recording without the hotkey path
+**I want** a performance trace to be created as a fallback when recording starts without an active trace
+**So that** direct invocation still gets traced
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If recording starts without an active trace, the system creates a trace before pipeline acquisition.
+- AC-2: The fallback trace is created even when the hotkey detection path was bypassed.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:199-260`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0787
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Record all trace milestones
+
+**As a** diagnostics consumer
+**I want** the active trace to record each session milestone timestamp
+**So that** performance timing can be measured across the full pipeline
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The active trace records the microphone live timestamp when the audio engine starts capturing.
+- AC-2: The active trace records the hotkey lifted timestamp when push-to-talk keys are released or toggle-to-talk is pressed again.
+- AC-3: The active trace records the microphone cold timestamp when the audio engine stops capturing.
+- AC-4: The active trace records the transcription start timestamp just before transcription begins.
+- AC-5: The active trace records the screen capture duration from the prefetch result's elapsed time.
+- AC-6: The active trace records the transcription end timestamp when transcription completes on both empty and non-empty paths.
+- AC-7: If refinement is attempted, the active trace records the refinement start timestamp when text refinement begins.
+- AC-8: If refinement is attempted, the active trace records the refinement end timestamp when text refinement completes.
+- AC-9: The active trace records the prompt build duration for prompt assembly.
+- AC-10: The active trace records the model inference duration for language model inference.
+- AC-11: The active trace records the post-processing duration for output post-processing.
+- AC-12: The active trace records the delivery start timestamp when text delivery begins.
+- AC-13: The active trace records the delivery end timestamp when text delivery completes.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:199-260`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0788
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Complete and clear the trace after delivery or empty transcription
+
+**As a** system finishing a recording session
+**I want** the trace to complete at the correct end condition and clean up active state
+**So that** finished sessions do not leak tracing state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The trace is completed after text delivery finishes or when an empty transcription is detected.
+- AC-2: If the delivery end timestamp was not already recorded, the completion path uses the current time as a fallback.
+- AC-3: After completion, the active trace is cleared.
+- AC-4: After completion, the refinement-attempted flag is reset.
+- AC-5: After completion, any pending screen capture prefetch is cancelled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:199-260`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0789
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Write the performance trace summary in the required format
+
+**As a** developer inspecting diagnostics
+**I want** the completed trace summary to be written to the debug log as a performance entry
+**So that** session timing and backend details are visible for debugging
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: On completion, the trace summary is written to the debug log as a performance entry.
+- AC-2: The summary includes the speech model identifier.
+- AC-3: The summary includes the refinement backend.
+- AC-4: The summary includes millisecond-formatted durations for the trace stages.
+- AC-5: The summary is a single space-separated string of key=value pairs.
+- AC-6: The summary includes session, speechModel, cleanupBackend, hotkey_to_mic_live, hotkey_lift_to_mic_cold, transcription, cleanup, paste, and total fields.
+- AC-7: When refinement is attempted, the summary includes ocr, prompt_build, model_call, and post_process fields.
+- AC-8: When refinement is not attempted, cleanup is reported as skipped.
+- AC-9: Missing values are displayed as n/a.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:199-260`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0790
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Abandon trace silently on acquisition or recording failure
+
+**As a** system handling startup failures
+**I want** the trace to be silently abandoned when pipeline acquisition or recording start fails
+**So that** failed sessions do not emit misleading performance summaries
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If pipeline acquisition fails, the trace is silently abandoned.
+- AC-2: If pipeline acquisition fails, no trace summary is written.
+- AC-3: If recording start fails because of an audio engine error, the trace is silently abandoned.
+- AC-4: If recording start fails because of an audio engine error, no trace summary is written.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:199-260`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0791
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Track performance trace identity and milestones
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** a trace structure with fixed identity and milestone fields
+**So that** pipeline timing can be recorded for each recording session
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A trace is an independent data structure copied on assignment rather than shared by reference.
+- AC-2: A trace is created with a new unique session identifier and a start timestamp that defaults to the current time.
+- AC-3: The trace defines ten milestone timestamps, all initially absent: Hotkey detected, Microphone live, Hotkey lifted, Microphone cold, Transcription start, Transcription end, Cleanup start, Cleanup end, Paste start, and Paste end.
+- AC-4: The trace defines four pre-computed durations, all initially absent: OCR capture duration, Prompt build duration, Model call duration, and Post-process duration.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:495-562`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0792
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Generate the performance trace summary string
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the trace to emit a fixed summary format
+**So that** performance data can be logged consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The summary is a single string of space-separated key=value pairs in the specified order: session, speechModel, cleanupBackend, hotkey_to_mic_live, hotkey_lift_to_mic_cold, transcription, cleanup, ocr, prompt_build, model_call, post_process, paste, total.
+- AC-2: When cleanup was not attempted, the cleanup, ocr, prompt_build, and model_call fields all show `skipped`.
+- AC-3: Duration values are formatted as integer milliseconds followed by `ms`.
+- AC-4: Converting seconds to milliseconds multiplies by 1000 and rounds to the nearest integer.
+- AC-5: If either endpoint for a computed duration is absent, the value is `n/a`.
+- AC-6: If a pre-computed duration is absent, the value is `n/a`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:495-562`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0793
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Manage performance trace lifecycle and completion
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** trace state transitions and completion handling to follow the specified lifecycle
+**So that** every recording session produces at most one completed summary
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A trace has four states: none, active, completed, and abandoned.
+- AC-2: A new trace is created at the start of every recording session, the hotkey-detected milestone is stamped immediately, and the cleanup-attempted flag is reset.
+- AC-3: If recording fails to start because the pipeline is busy or errors, the trace is discarded silently.
+- AC-4: Milestones are stamped at the corresponding pipeline events as recording progresses through audio capture, transcription, cleanup, and paste.
+- AC-5: Completion occurs after paste completes or when an empty transcription is detected.
+- AC-6: If paste-end has not yet been stamped at completion time, it is stamped with the current time as a fallback.
+- AC-7: On completion, the summary is recorded as a Performance category log entry, then the trace is cleared and the cleanup-attempted flag is reset.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:495-562`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0794
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Emit performance traces
+
+**As a** debugging user
+**I want** each completed dictation to produce a performance summary line
+**So that** I can identify the slow stage in the pipeline
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each completed dictation produces a single space-separated `key=value` performance trace line.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/debug-investigation.md:1-138`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0795
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Include performance metrics
+
+**As a** debugging user
+**I want** the trace to include the documented pipeline metrics
+**So that** I can compare stage durations directly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The performance trace contains `session`, `speechModel`, `cleanupBackend`, `hotkey_to_mic_live`, `hotkey_lift_to_mic_cold`, `transcription`, `cleanup`, `ocr`, `prompt_build`, `model_call`, `post_process`, `paste`, and `total` fields.
+- AC-2: `cleanup` and `ocr` may appear as either `<N>ms` or `skipped`.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/debug-investigation.md:1-138`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0796
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Render missing performance values as n/a
+
+**As a** debugging user
+**I want** missing performance values to be shown as n/a
+**So that** I can tell when a stage did not produce a measurement
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Missing performance values display as `n/a` in the trace output.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/debug-investigation.md:1-138`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0797
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Create a performance trace at hotkey detection
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** a new performance trace to be created when the hotkey is detected
+**So that** the session can record timing for each stage
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A new performance trace is created with a unique session identifier and the hotkey-detected timestamp is stamped immediately.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:35-36`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0798
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-025 — Performance Tracing
+**Title:** Complete the performance trace
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the trace to be finalized after paste completes
+**So that** timing data is logged and temporary state is cleared
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The paste-end callback fires and stamps the paste-end timestamp.
+- AC-2: The trace summary is written to the debug log as a performance-category entry with millisecond-formatted durations for each stage.
+- AC-3: The trace is cleared.
+- AC-4: Any still-pending OCR prefetch is cancelled.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:168-171`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving

+## STORY-0800
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Serialize and deserialize raw Float32 audio
+
+**As a** consumer of saved audio samples
+**I want** raw Float32 serialization to preserve sample data exactly
+**So that** audio can round-trip without loss
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Raw serialization converts the float sample array directly to bytes via memory copy.
+- AC-2: Raw deserialization rejects data whose length is not an exact multiple of 4 bytes.
+- AC-3: Invalid raw data length reports an "invalid serialized audio data" error.
+- AC-4: Raw serialization round-trips losslessly.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:374-425`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0801
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Serialize audio to valid WAV files
+
+**As a** user exporting playable audio
+**I want** WAV serialization to produce a standards-compliant file in memory
+**So that** recordings can be played or archived
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: WAV serialization produces a complete valid WAV file in memory.
+- AC-2: The WAV file uses a 16,000 Hz sample rate, mono channel count, 16-bit signed integer PCM, and audio format code 1.
+- AC-3: The file begins with a RIFF chunk header and a chunk size equal to 36 plus the data payload size.
+- AC-4: The file includes a WAVE format identifier.
+- AC-5: The file includes a fmt sub-chunk with a 16-byte payload containing the PCM format code, channel count, sample rate, byte rate, block alignment, and bits per sample in little-endian order.
+- AC-6: The file includes a data sub-chunk with the sample payload.
+- AC-7: During WAV serialization, each float sample is clamped to [-1.0, 1.0], scaled by 32767, rounded to the nearest integer, and written as little-endian signed int16.
+- AC-8: WAV round-trip preserves sample values within a tolerance of 0.0001.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:374-425`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0802
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Deserialize audio by auto-detecting WAV or raw format
+
+**As a** consumer of serialized audio
+**I want** the deserializer to auto-detect the input format and validate WAV structure
+**So that** both raw and WAV payloads can be loaded safely
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the data begins with the bytes "RIFF", the deserializer parses it as WAV.
+- AC-2: If the data does not begin with "RIFF", the deserializer parses it as raw Float32.
+- AC-3: WAV deserialization rejects inputs shorter than 44 bytes.
+- AC-4: WAV deserialization rejects inputs that do not start with "RIFF".
+- AC-5: WAV deserialization rejects inputs whose bytes 8 through 11 are not "WAVE".
+- AC-6: Sub-chunk parsing begins at byte offset 12 and iterates through chunks.
+- AC-7: Each sub-chunk is read as a 4-byte ASCII identifier and a 4-byte little-endian size.
+- AC-8: If a chunk's data extends beyond the end of the input, an error is reported.
+- AC-9: Odd-sized chunks must be followed by one padding byte.
+- AC-10: When the fmt sub-chunk is encountered, it must be at least 16 bytes.
+- AC-11: The deserializer extracts the audio format code, channel count, and bits per sample from the fmt sub-chunk.
+- AC-12: After parsing, the deserializer validates that the format code is 1, the channel count is 1, the bits per sample is 16, and the sample data byte count is even.
+- AC-13: Any validation failure reports an "invalid serialized audio data" error.
+- AC-14: Integer-to-float conversion divides each little-endian signed 16-bit sample by 32767.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:374-425`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0803
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Store transcription lab sessions and files
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** transcription lab data to be stored with the documented index, timings, and audio layout
+**So that** recording sessions can be reprocessed later
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The transcription lab storage directory is `<Application Support>/GhostPepper/transcription-lab/` by default.
+- AC-2: The index file is `transcription-lab-index.json` and stores an array of entry objects with the documented required fields.
+- AC-3: The `windowContents` field encodes to plain text and decodes back into an OCR context when a string is present.
+- AC-4: A null or absent `windowContents` decodes as no context.
+- AC-5: The timings file is `transcription-lab-timings.json` and stores a dictionary keyed by entry UUID strings.
+- AC-6: Timing objects contain optional transcription-duration and cleanup-duration values expressed as floating-point seconds.
+- AC-7: Invalid UUID keys in the timings file are discarded when loading.
+- AC-8: Audio files are stored in the `audio/` directory and are named by the entry's audio file name.
+- AC-9: Audio files are WAV files in 16 kHz, mono, 16-bit signed integer PCM format.
+- AC-10: Loading entries from a missing index file returns an empty list.
+- AC-11: Loaded entries are sorted by creation timestamp with newest first.
+- AC-12: Loading timings from a missing timings file returns an empty dictionary.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:273-378`
+
+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0804
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Insert and prune transcription lab entries
+
+**As a** developer
+**I want** entry insertion to follow the documented ordering, pruning, and write sequence
+**So that** the transcription lab store stays consistent on disk
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Inserting an entry creates the audio directory if needed before writing audio.
+- AC-2: The audio data file is written atomically.
+- AC-3: The existing index is loaded from disk before the new entry is merged.
+- AC-4: Any existing entry with the same identifier is removed before the new one is added.
+- AC-5: After insertion, entries are sorted by creation timestamp with newest first.
+- AC-6: Existing timing data is loaded and the new entry's timing record is added.
+- AC-7: When more than 50 entries exist, entries beyond the limit are pruned after sorting.
+- AC-8: Pruning deletes each pruned entry's audio file and removes its timing record.
+- AC-9: Pruning writes the pruned index and timings files back to disk.
+- AC-10: The pruned index JSON and timings JSON are written atomically.
+- AC-11: Deleting an audio file during pruning ignores errors silently.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:273-378`
+
+**Status:** pending
+

+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving

+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** recordings to be archived after live sessions complete
+**So that** every session can be replayed later
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Archiving is skipped entirely when the audio buffer is empty.
+- AC-2: Audio is serialized in WAV format and named with the entry identifier plus `.wav`.
+- AC-3: Duration is computed from sample count divided by sample rate at 16,000 Hz.
+- AC-4: Transcription and cleanup durations are extracted from the active performance trace.
+- AC-5: If cleanup is enabled, the cleanup model display name is recorded.
+- AC-6: If cleanup is disabled, the literal string `Cleanup disabled` is recorded.
+- AC-7: The speaker-filtering-used-fallback field is derived from the diarization summary fallback flag and defaults to false when no summary exists.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0832
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Archive sessions after failed transcription or successful cleanup
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** live sessions to be archived at the documented points in the pipeline
+**So that** sessions with and without successful transcription still produce the right archived state
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Recording sessions are archived after every session, including sessions that produce no transcription.
+- AC-2: A failed transcription archives nil raw and corrected text.
+- AC-3: A successful cleanup archives full results.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:693-694`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0833
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Log archival failures without blocking the pipeline
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** archive errors to be non-fatal
+**So that** the live pipeline keeps running even if archival fails
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Archival errors are logged.
+- AC-2: Archival errors do not cause the pipeline to fail.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:564-714`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0834
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-026 — Recording History & Archiving
+**Title:** Archive the recording session
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** the recording artifacts to be serialized into the transcription lab archive
+**So that** the session can be reviewed later
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The audio buffer, OCR context, raw transcription, corrected transcription, and diarization summary are serialized and stored in the transcription lab archive.
+- AC-2: Audio is serialized to WAV format at 16kHz mono 16-bit PCM.
+- AC-3: Capacity management prunes archive entries beyond 50.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/normal-dictation.md:141-143`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+
+**Summary:** App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Stories:** STORY-0835, STORY-0836, STORY-0837, STORY-0838, STORY-0839, STORY-0840, STORY-0841, STORY-0842, STORY-0843, STORY-0844, STORY-0845, STORY-0846, STORY-0847, STORY-0848, STORY-0849, STORY-0850, STORY-0851, STORY-0852, STORY-0853, STORY-0854, STORY-0855, STORY-0856, STORY-0857
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md`, `specs/specs/domains/input.md`, `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md`
+**Status:** 0/23 done
+
+## STORY-0835
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Block re-entrant recording and stop requests
+
+**As a** recording session
+**I want** recording and stop-and-transcribe entry points guarded against concurrent re-entry
+**So that** the pipeline cannot start duplicate work
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If transcription is already in progress, a second stop-and-transcribe request is silently ignored.
+- AC-2: The transcription guard is cleared when transcription completes, regardless of success or failure.
+- AC-3: Recording does not start unless the application status is Ready.
+- AC-4: Attempts to start recording from any status other than Ready are silently ignored, and the Loading model overlay is shown when the status is Loading.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:162-175`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0836
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Run first-appearance initialization after onboarding
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** initialization to run after onboarding and perform all required startup checks and setup
+**So that** the application is ready for recording on first appearance
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If onboarding has not been completed, initialization does not proceed until onboarding completes.
+- AC-2: On first appearance after onboarding, the system checks microphone permission and accessibility plus input monitoring permissions.
+- AC-3: If microphone permission or accessibility/input monitoring permission is missing, system settings are opened.
+- AC-4: On first appearance after onboarding, the audio engine is pre-warmed, paste target tracking starts, the speech model is loaded with a model-loading overlay, the hotkey monitor starts, and the refinement model state is refreshed.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:558-574`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0837
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Show onboarding before initialization
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** onboarding to appear before startup initialization when it has not been completed
+**So that** setup is completed in the right order
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If onboarding has not been completed, the onboarding flow is shown first.
+- AC-2: Initialization begins only after onboarding completes.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:558-574`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0838
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Clean up on termination
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** termination to stop background work cleanly
+**So that** the app shuts down without leaving pending work behind
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: On application termination or the will-terminate notification, OCR or screen capture prefetch is cancelled.
+- AC-2: On application termination or the will-terminate notification, the cleanup or refinement backend is shut down.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:558-574`
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:798-816`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0839
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Toggle automatic termination with application lifecycle
+
+**As a** system
+**I want** automatic termination to be disabled while the menu bar app is active and re-enabled at shutdown
+**So that** the app stays alive when idle but exits cleanly on termination
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Automatic termination is disabled at application startup.
+- AC-2: Automatic termination is re-enabled at termination.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/core-pipeline.md:558-574`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0840
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Guard mutable monitor state with one lock
+
+**As a** concurrency layer
+**I want** all mutable monitoring state to be protected by a single lock
+**So that** state transitions stay consistent across threads
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: All mutable monitoring state is protected by a single lock.
+- AC-2: The lock is held during state evaluation, start, stop, binding updates, and suspension changes.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:299-307`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0841
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Serialize tap events off the tap thread
+
+**As a** event processing system
+**I want** tap callbacks to forward events to a serial processing queue
+**So that** chord evaluation does not run on the tap thread
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Events from the tap callback are forwarded to a serial processing queue.
+- AC-2: Chord evaluation is kept off the tap thread.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:299-307`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0842
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Keep engine state unsynchronized internally
+
+**As a** concurrency layer
+**I want** the chord engine and shortcut capture state to rely on the monitor lock for thread safety
+**So that** they do not expose separate synchronization behavior
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The chord engine has no internal synchronization.
+- AC-2: The shortcut capture state has no internal synchronization.
+- AC-3: Their thread safety comes from the monitor's lock.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:299-307`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0843
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Run initialization in the specified order
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** startup to follow the specified permission-aware sequence
+**So that** the app initializes predictably and stops only when it should
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When permission prompts are not being skipped, initialization registers launch at login first, then checks microphone permission, then checks accessibility and input monitoring, then pre-warms the audio engine, then starts focus tracking, then loads the speech model, then starts the hotkey monitor, and then refreshes the cleanup model state.
+- AC-2: If microphone is denied, initialization halts with an error before later startup steps run.
+- AC-3: If accessibility or input monitoring is missing, the app opens the settings window and continues instead of halting.
+- AC-4: The hotkey monitor start step may prompt for accessibility if it fails and may prompt for input monitoring if that permission is not granted.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:173-188`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0844
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Skip permission prompts after onboarding
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** permission prompts to be bypassed after onboarding has already acquired them
+**So that** post-onboarding startup does not repeat permissions work
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: In skip permission prompts mode, the microphone check is bypassed.
+- AC-2: In skip permission prompts mode, the accessibility and input monitoring checks are bypassed.
+- AC-3: This mode is used when initialization follows onboarding and permissions were already acquired.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:173-188`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0845
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Register launch at login only on first run
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** launch-at-login registration to happen only on first run
+**So that** subsequent startups do not repeat first-run setup work
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: During initialization, launch-at-login registration occurs as the first step.
+- AC-2: Launch-at-login registration is performed only on the first run.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:175-177`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0846
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Register launch at login only once on first run
+
+**As a** application
+**I want** first-run launch-at-login registration to run only once
+**So that** login item registration is not repeated automatically after setup
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If `hasSetLaunchAtLogin` is not `true` on first run, the app attempts launch-at-login registration.
+- AC-2: After that first-run registration attempt, the app sets `hasSetLaunchAtLogin` to `true`.
+- AC-3: Once `hasSetLaunchAtLogin` is `true`, the app does not automatically repeat registration.
+- AC-4: Registration errors are suppressed silently.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:190-271`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0847
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Mirror launch-at-login service state in settings
+
+**As a** settings user
+**I want** the launch-at-login toggle to reflect and control the service registration state
+**So that** I can enable or disable login launch from settings
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The toggle initial state is set by querying whether the main app service status is enabled.
+- AC-2: Enabling the toggle calls the service register function.
+- AC-3: Disabling the toggle calls the service unregister function.
+- AC-4: If either service operation throws an error, the toggle reverts to its previous state.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:380-392`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0848
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Control automatic termination on app lifecycle events
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** automatic termination to be disabled while the app is active and re-enabled on termination
+**So that** the menu bar app stays alive when idle
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: On appearance, the app disables automatic termination with the stated reason.
+- AC-2: On the will-terminate notification, the app re-enables automatic termination.
+- AC-3: The behavior prevents the system from killing the process for inactivity.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:798-816`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0849
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Run initialization exactly once
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** startup initialization to be guarded against repeated appearance events
+**So that** the app does not initialize multiple times
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: A boolean flag ensures the initialization logic runs exactly once.
+- AC-2: Repeated appearance handler calls do not repeat initialization.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:798-816`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0850
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Lazily initialize update checking
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the auto-update controller to initialize only when needed
+**So that** startup cost stays low if update checking is never used
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The auto-update controller is deferred until first access.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:798-816`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0851
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Reuse hidden windows
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** window managers to reuse existing windows instead of creating new ones every time
+**So that** window state is preserved and duplicates are avoided
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Each window manager stores a single optional window reference.
+- AC-2: Showing a window reuses an existing window by bringing it to the front when one exists.
+- AC-3: Showing a window creates a new one when no reference exists.
+- AC-4: Windows are configured not to be released when closed.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0852
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Hide windows on close
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** closing a window to hide it instead of destroying it
+**So that** the window remains available for reuse
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Closing a window hides it rather than destroying it.
+- AC-2: A hidden window remains available for reuse.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0853
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Drive window display from central app state
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** window display to be triggered through central application state
+**So that** all windows are coordinated consistently
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The central application state holds references to all window managers.
+- AC-2: The central application state exposes show methods that trigger window display.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0854
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Commit edits before hiding text windows
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the prompt editor and cleanup transcript windows to resign first responder before hiding
+**So that** in-progress edits are committed
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The prompt editor window resigns first responder before hiding.
+- AC-2: The cleanup transcript window resigns first responder before hiding.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0855
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Configure the recording overlay panel
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the recording overlay to use the documented panel behavior
+**So that** it does not interfere with normal app interaction
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The recording overlay panel uses non-activating and borderless style.
+- AC-2: The recording overlay panel ignores mouse events.
+- AC-3: The recording overlay panel joins all spaces.
+- AC-4: The recording overlay panel participates as full-screen auxiliary.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0856
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Configure the debug log panel
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the debug log panel to use the documented window behavior
+**So that** it stays accessible during app changes
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The debug log panel uses utility window style.
+- AC-2: The debug log panel behaves as a floating window.
+- AC-3: The debug log panel does not hide on deactivation.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0857
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-027 — App Lifecycle & Window Management
+**Title:** Handle onboarding window completion uniquely
+
+**As a** transcription lab user
+**I want** the onboarding window to be shown and torn down with its special lifecycle
+**So that** setup completes cleanly and the window is not reused afterward
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Before showing, the onboarding window sets the activation policy to regular.
+- AC-2: On completion, the onboarding window is closed.
+- AC-3: On completion, the onboarding window reference is released instead of preserved for reuse.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:818-830`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+
+**Summary:** Dictation Toggle
+**Stories:** STORY-0858, STORY-0859, STORY-0860, STORY-0861, STORY-0862, STORY-0863, STORY-0864, STORY-0865, STORY-0866, STORY-0867, STORY-0868, STORY-0869, STORY-0870, STORY-0871
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/domains/input.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md`
+**Status:** 0/14 done
+
+## STORY-0858
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Toggle recording with the full chord
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** recording to start and stop by pressing the full toggle chord
+**So that** I can keep recording on without holding keys down
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Recording begins when the user presses the complete toggle-to-talk chord.
+- AC-2: Once active, the user may release keys and type normally without affecting recording.
+- AC-3: Recording stops only when the exact toggle chord is pressed a second time.
+- AC-4: Between the start and stop activations, all key events that do not produce an exact toggle-chord match are ignored.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:144-150`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0859
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Upgrade push mode to toggle mode
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** push-to-talk to upgrade into toggle-to-talk when I complete the longer chord
+**So that** I can switch modes mid-recording without stopping
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: While push-to-talk is active, pressing additional keys to complete the toggle-to-talk chord upgrades the recording mode to toggle-to-talk.
+- AC-2: The upgrade discards the current recording buffer and starts a fresh recording in toggle mode.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/input.md:152-158`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0860
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Start toggle recording on exact chord
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the toggle-to-talk chord to start recording
+**So that** I can begin dictation without holding keys
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Pressing Right Command + Right Option + Space while idle starts toggle-to-talk recording.
+- AC-2: When toggle recording starts, the overlay shows "Recording..." with a pulsing red dot.
+- AC-3: Starting toggle recording creates a performance trace and stamps the hotkey-detected timestamp.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:1-40`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0861
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Keep toggle recording active through releases
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** toggle recording to continue after I release the keys
+**So that** I can speak or type normally while recording stays active
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Releasing all keys while toggle-to-talk is active does not stop recording.
+- AC-2: Typing normal keystrokes in the foreground application does not affect the recording.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:20-60`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0862
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Stop toggle recording on second chord press
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the same chord to stop toggle recording
+**So that** I can end dictation hands-free
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Pressing Right Command + Right Option + Space again while toggle-to-talk is active stops recording and returns the engine to idle.
+- AC-2: Stopping toggle recording fires the stop callback and stamps the hotkey-lifted timestamp.
+- AC-3: After stop, transcription, cleanup, and paste proceed exactly as they do in normal dictation.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:20-60`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0863
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Start push-to-talk recording
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the push-to-talk chord to start recording
+**So that** I can begin dictation by holding the shortcut
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Holding Right Command + Right Option starts push-to-talk active recording.
+- AC-2: Starting push-to-talk produces a start effect and begins recording using the normal dictation sequence.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:61-95`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0864
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Upgrade push-to-talk into toggle mode
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** to add Space while holding push-to-talk and convert it into toggle mode
+**So that** I can switch to hands-free recording without restarting manually
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: While Right Command + Right Option is held, pressing Space creates an exact match for toggle-to-talk.
+- AC-2: When that exact match occurs during push-to-talk recording, the mode upgrades to toggle-to-talk and a restart recording effect is produced.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:61-110`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0865
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Handle audio buffer on push-to-toggle upgrade
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the upgrade path to keep or clear pre-upgrade audio based on how much was captured
+**So that** the resulting transcription contains the intended audio span
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If fewer than 16,000 samples have been accumulated at upgrade time, the audio buffer is cleared and the discarded sample count is logged.
+- AC-2: If 16,000 or more samples have accumulated, the buffer is preserved and the log records that existing audio is being kept.
+- AC-3: Upgrading within the first second transcribes only audio after the upgrade, while upgrading after more than one second transcribes the full recording together.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:73-120`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0866
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Ignore non-chord keys during toggle recording
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** toggle recording to ignore unrelated key presses
+**So that** normal typing and other shortcuts do not interrupt dictation
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: While toggle-to-talk is active, key events that do not form an exact toggle-to-talk match are ignored by the chord engine.
+- AC-2: Foreground typing continues to work normally while toggle recording stays active.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/toggle-dictation.md:20-150`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0867
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-028 — Dictation Toggle
+**Title:** Fall back to push-to-talk when toggle is unavailable
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** push-to-talk to remain available when no toggle chord is configured
+**So that** I can still dictate even without the toggle shortcut
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If only push-to-talk is configured and no toggle-to-talk chord exists, toggle-to-talk behavior is unavailable.

+## STORY-0872
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-029 — Onboarding & First Run
+**Title:** Gate onboarding on completion state

+**As a** new user

+**So that** first-launch setup runs once and then hands off to the app
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The onboarding wizard is shown when the `onboardingCompleted` UserDefaults key is false on a fresh install.
+- AC-2: Completing onboarding sets `onboardingCompleted` to true.
+- AC-3: After onboarding is completed, application initialization proceeds.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:95-139`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0887

+**Epic:** EPIC-029 — Onboarding & First Run

+**So that** I can verify audio input before continuing
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The setup step includes a Microphone row.
+- AC-2: When microphone access is neither granted nor denied, a "Grant" button triggers the system permission prompt.
+- AC-3: When microphone access is granted, the view loads input devices and starts microphone level monitoring.
+- AC-4: When microphone access is denied, an "Open Settings" button replaces the grant button.
+- AC-5: After access is granted, a device picker appears when more than one device is available.
+- AC-6: After access is granted, a sound level meter appears alongside the device picker.
+- AC-7: The level meter is green normally, orange above 30%, and red above 70%.
+- AC-8: Changing the selected device restarts the level monitor on the new device.
+- AC-9: The microphone level monitor starts only if permission is already authorized.
+- AC-10: The microphone level monitor installs an audio tap on input bus 0 with buffer size 1024.
+- AC-11: The microphone level monitor computes RMS from float channel data.
+- AC-12: The microphone level monitor normalizes the level to the 0-1 range by multiplying by 10 and clamping.
+- AC-13: When the microphone level monitor stops, it removes the audio tap, stops the engine, and resets the level to zero.

+## STORY-0910
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-029 — Onboarding & First Run
+**Title:** Run the try-it recording flow
+
+**As a** new user
+**I want** a guided hotkey-and-recording test
+**So that** I can confirm Ghost Pepper captures and transcribes my voice
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The Try It screen instructs the user to hold Right Command + Right Option and say something.
+- AC-2: The Try It screen displays key cap widgets for both keys.
+- AC-3: A dedicated hotkey monitor is created with the default push-to-talk chord Right Command + Right Option.
+- AC-4: If the hotkey monitor fails to start, it retries up to 5 times at 2-second intervals.
+- AC-5: While the chord is held, the key caps turn red and a fresh audio recorder starts capturing microphone input in 16kHz mono float32 format.
+- AC-6: When the keys are released, recording stops after the 200 ms flush delay.
+- AC-7: After recording stops, the audio buffer is transcribed using the loaded speech model.
+- AC-8: If transcription produces text, the result is displayed for 2 seconds with the success message: It works! Your words will be pasted wherever your cursor is.
+- AC-9: After a successful transcription, the step auto-advances to Done and does not advance more than once.
+- AC-10: Skip and Continue buttons are always available on the Try It step.

+- `specs/specs/journeys/first-run.md:1-222`

+**Status:** pending

+- AC-1: The Done screen displays a green checkmark icon, the heading You're All Set!, a menu bar mockup, a capability list, and a Start Using Ghost Pepper button.
+- AC-2: Clicking Start Using Ghost Pepper sets the onboarding-completed flag to true in user defaults.
+- AC-3: Clicking Start Using Ghost Pepper fires the completion callback and triggers full application initialization.
+- AC-4: After completion, the application reverts to menu-bar-only mode with no Dock icon.

+

+**Title:** Skip onboarding after completion

+**So that** I go straight to the normal application on later launches
+

+- `specs/specs/journeys/first-run.md:173-173`

+**Status:** pending

+## STORY-0913
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-029 — Onboarding & First Run
+**Title:** Initialize the app after onboarding
+
+**As a** newly onboarded user
+**I want** the application to initialize its normal runtime services after onboarding
+**So that** the app is ready for regular use
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Launch-at-login is registered on first run and guarded by a preference flag so it runs only once.
+- AC-2: Microphone permission is verified and permission prompts are suppressed because permissions were just acquired during onboarding.
+- AC-3: The audio engine is pre-warmed to reduce first-recording latency.
+- AC-4: Paste target focus tracking starts and begins observing the frontmost application.
+- AC-5: The speech model is confirmed loaded or loading resumes.
+- AC-6: The hotkey monitor starts and installs the global event tap.
+- AC-7: The cleanup model state is refreshed.
+- AC-8: Application status transitions from loading to ready once both the speech model has loaded and the hotkey monitor has started.
+- AC-9: The auto-updater is lazily initialized when the full menu bar view is first displayed.
+- AC-10: The menu bar dropdown shows the full set of controls: Settings, Debug Log, version, Check for Updates, and Quit.
+- AC-11: Automatic update checks are enabled at a 24-hour interval.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/first-run.md:1-222`
+
+**Status:** pending

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-029 — Onboarding & First Run

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/first-run.md:1-222`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-030 — Settings UI
+
+**Summary:** Settings UI
+**Stories:** STORY-0915, STORY-0916, STORY-0917, STORY-0918, STORY-0919, STORY-0920, STORY-0921, STORY-0922, STORY-0923, STORY-0924, STORY-0925, STORY-0926, STORY-0927, STORY-0928, STORY-0929, STORY-0930, STORY-0931, STORY-0932, STORY-0933, STORY-0934, STORY-0935, STORY-0936, STORY-0937, STORY-0938, STORY-0939, STORY-0940, STORY-0941, STORY-0942, STORY-0943, STORY-0944, STORY-0945, STORY-0946, STORY-0947, STORY-0948, STORY-0949, STORY-0950, STORY-0951, STORY-0952, STORY-0953, STORY-0954, STORY-0955, STORY-0956, STORY-0957

+**Status:** 0/43 done

+## STORY-0915
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-030 — Settings UI
+**Title:** Refresh settings-window permission state
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the settings window to keep its permission state current
+**So that** it reflects changes made in System Settings
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: The settings window refreshes accessibility, input monitoring, and screen recording permission states when it appears.
+- AC-2: The settings window refreshes those permission states when the application becomes active after returning from System Settings.
+- AC-3: If accessibility or input monitoring is not granted, the settings window starts a repeating timer at 2-second intervals to poll both permissions.
+- AC-4: The polling timer stops when both permissions are granted.
+- AC-5: The polling timer stops when the settings window disappears.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:141-161`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0916
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-030 — Settings UI
+**Title:** Show the permissions card in settings
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a permission status card in the Recording settings section when access is missing
+**So that** I can see what is granted and what still needs attention
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If either accessibility or input monitoring permission is missing, the Recording settings section shows a Permissions card.
+- AC-2: Each permission row shows a green checkmark icon when granted.
+- AC-3: Each permission row shows a red X icon when denied.
+- AC-4: Each denied permission row shows a Grant button.
+- AC-5: The accessibility Grant button invokes the prompting accessibility check.
+- AC-6: The input monitoring Grant button invokes both the input monitoring prompt and opens the Input Monitoring settings pane.
+- AC-7: The explanatory text below the rows reads `Both permissions are required for hotkeys and pasting to work reliably. If Ghost Pepper doesn't appear in Input Monitoring, click + and select it from Applications.`
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:141-161`
+
+**Status:** pending
+

+
+**Epic:** EPIC-030 — Settings UI

+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the Permissions card to disappear once the required permissions are granted
+**So that** the settings view stays uncluttered
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When both accessibility and input monitoring are granted, the entire Permissions card is hidden from the Recording section.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:141-161`
+
+**Status:** pending

+# EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+
+**Summary:** Cleanup Model Management
+**Stories:** STORY-0958, STORY-0959, STORY-0960, STORY-0961, STORY-0962, STORY-0963, STORY-0964, STORY-0965, STORY-0966, STORY-0967, STORY-0968, STORY-0969, STORY-0970, STORY-0971
+**Primary sources:** `specs/specs/domains/system.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md`, `specs/specs/journeys/settings-customization.md`
+**Status:** 0/14 done
+
+## STORY-0958
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Show cleanup download progress
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** cleanup model downloads to report progress
+**So that** long downloads are visible while they run
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Cleanup model download progress transitions from 0.0 to 1.0 while the file is being fetched.
+- AC-2: The menu bar dropdown shows a linear progress bar for the cleanup model download.
+- AC-3: The Runtime models card shows active download text for the cleanup model.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/system.md:433-481`
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0959
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Handle cleanup model switches through state transitions
+
+**As a** user changing the cleanup model picker
+**I want** the cleanup model state machine to react to the current loaded model
+**So that** switching to a new cleanup model follows the expected transition path
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: When a different cleanup model is already loaded, selecting a new cleanup model transitions the cleanup model state from Ready into a download or load path for the new model.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0960
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Check cleanup model cache by GGUF file
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** cleanup model cache status checked from the models directory
+**So that** download decisions use the on-disk GGUF file
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Cleanup model cache status is determined by checking for the GGUF file under the application's cleanup models subdirectory.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0961
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Download uncached cleanup models from HuggingFace
+
+**As a** user selecting an uncached cleanup model
+**I want** the model to download from HuggingFace
+**So that** I can use a cleanup model that is not already on disk
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If the selected cleanup model is not cached, the app begins downloading it from HuggingFace.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0962
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Move downloaded cleanup models into the models directory
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** downloaded cleanup models moved into the models directory
+**So that** the downloaded file is stored in the expected location
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After a cleanup model download finishes, the file is moved to the models directory.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0963
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Load cleanup models asynchronously with the configured parameters
+
+**As a** the application
+**I want** cleanup model initialization to run in the background with the required settings
+**So that** loading does not block the foreground flow and the model is configured correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Cleanup model initialization runs asynchronously in the background.
+- AC-2: Cleanup model initialization uses temperature 0.1, thinking mode suppressed, and the default cleanup system prompt.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0964
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Mark cleanup models ready after successful load
+
+**As a** user loading a cleanup model
+**I want** the cleanup model state to become ready on success
+**So that** cleanup can use the loaded model
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After successful cleanup model initialization, the cleanup model state transitions to ready.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0965
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Show cleanup download errors in the settings card
+
+**As a** user whose cleanup model download failed
+**I want** the error shown in the Cleanup settings card
+**So that** I can see why the model did not load
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If a cleanup model download fails, the cleanup model state transitions to error with a message.
+- AC-2: The error message is displayed in the Cleanup settings card.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0966
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Fall back to the default cleanup model for unknown preferences
+
+**As a** user with an invalid stored cleanup model preference
+**I want** the app to use the default cleanup model instead
+**So that** cleanup remains available even when the stored preference is invalid
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If a stored cleanup model preference refers to an unknown model identifier, the system falls back to the default 0.8B cleanup model.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0967
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Queue cleanup load requests while another load is in progress
+
+**As a** user requesting a cleanup model load during an active load
+**I want** the request to wait until the current load resolves
+**So that** cleanup loading stays serialized
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: If a cleanup model load is requested while another load is already in progress, the requester waits until the loading state resolves.
+- AC-2: If the loaded model matches the requested model, the requester proceeds.
+- AC-3: If the loaded model does not match the requested model, a new load begins.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:1-179`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0968
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Transition cleanup models to loading before initialization
+
+**As a** user downloading a cleanup model
+**I want** the cleanup model to enter loading state before initialization begins
+**So that** the UI and state machine reflect the load phase correctly
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: After a cleanup model download finishes and the file is moved to the models directory, the model state transitions to loading before asynchronous initialization runs.
+
+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/journeys/model-management.md:72-79`
+
+**Status:** pending
+
+## STORY-0969
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-031 — Cleanup Model Management
+**Title:** Show cleanup model choices
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** the cleanup model picker to list the available cleanup models
+**So that** I can choose among the supported sizes
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**

+# EPIC-032 — Window Management & Utility Views

+## STORY-0972
+
+**Epic:** EPIC-032 — Window Management & Utility Views
+**Title:** Open the cleanup prompt editor as a reusable window
+
+**As a** user
+**I want** a dedicated cleanup prompt editor window with the specified behavior
+**So that** I can edit the cleanup prompt without reopening a new window each time
+
+**Acceptance criteria:**
+- AC-1: Opening the prompt editor presents a window titled `Edit Cleanup Prompt` with an initial size of 500 x 400 points and titled, closable, resizable styling.
+- AC-2: Closing the editor hides it instead of destroying it, and text edits are committed before the window hides.
+- AC-3: If the editor is already open, the app reuses the existing window.

+**Sources:**
+- `specs/specs/domains/user-experience.md:307-323`

+**Status:** pending

 - Why this matters: The extraction skill requires passing chunk content inline to subagents rather than having them read files. In Codex, that makes the workflow sensitive to tool-output token limits during prompt staging, and partial payloads are easy to miss until the extracted stories under-cover the source.
 - Workaround used: Discard the affected extraction result, restage each batch individually with enough output budget to include the full payload, and only then dispatch the subagent.
 - Suggested fix: Add a documented Codex-specific staging method for extraction payloads that avoids silent truncation, or provide a helper script that emits one batch payload per call with an integrity marker so the orchestrator can verify completeness before dispatch.
+
+### PAR review steps assume subagent delegation that this session policy can forbid
+
+- Phase: `scoping-the-simplest-core` and later review checkpoints
+- Trigger: Reaching the roadmap scope-review step after extraction while operating under a tool policy that only permits `spawn_agent` when Jesse explicitly asks for delegation.
+- Observed behavior: The iterative-development skills require PAR reviewer subagents, but the active tool guardrail blocks that mechanism for an autonomous run unless the user has explicitly requested subagents.
+- Why this matters: The skills currently treat PAR as a hard gate, so the documented happy path can become unavailable even when the user asked for uninterrupted autonomous execution.
+- Workaround used: Record the constraint, then perform the same review intent locally in multiple adversarial passes instead of dispatching reviewer agents.
+- Suggested fix: Document a first-class local fallback for PAR steps when delegation is unavailable, so the methodology remains usable without violating tool policy.
